Foreign key linking to plan.plan_service_area. Business justification: Network adequacy and service‑area compliance reports need to map each claim to the plan’s service area where the service was rendered.

business_glossary_term: Claiming Provider Provider Provider Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to provider.provider_provider. Business justification: Required for network participation and quality reporting; each claim must be linked to the provider entity for regulatory and performance analytics.

Foreign key linking to care.care_program. Business justification: Needed for Program ROI analysis; associates each claim with the care program enrollment at service time for financial and outcome reporting.
Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Needed for Claim Intake Eligibility Check: validates rendering provider's credential status before claim acceptance.

Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to apply network‑specific contracts and cost‑share rules; claim processors need to know the provider network for each claim.
Foreign key linking to contract.party. Business justification: Needed to associate each claim with the contracting party (provider organization) for regulatory reporting and payment reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Regulatory submissions aggregate claims; associating claim with its submission supports submission tracking and audit of filing completeness.

FK to provider.provider.provider_id — Every claim must resolve to a rendering provider for network status determination, fee schedule lookup, and provider payment. This is the second most critical operational join in health insurance.

Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: Regulatory and solvency reporting aggregates claims by risk pool; linking claim to its pool enables accurate financial and compliance analysis.

FK to member.subscriber.subscriber_id — The claim-to-member join is the single most critical operational query in health insurance. Every claim must resolve to a member for eligibility verification, accumulator updates, and EOB generation.

Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_transaction. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to reference the exact enrollment transaction that established coverage at service date, used in CMS audit and eligibility verification reports.

Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Needed for Case Management Dashboard; each claim must be tied to the utilization case that governs its clinical and financial review.
Foreign key linking to plan.plan_year. Business justification: Regulatory reporting and rate filing require associating each claim with the specific plan year under which coverage was provided.

Indicates whether this claim line was adjudicated during the Part D coverage gap (donut hole) phase. Used for manufacturer discount program tracking and CMS Part D benefit phase reporting.

Number of days of medication supply dispensed on this claim line as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for refill-too-soon edits, adherence calculations, and HEDIS medication adherence measures.

NCPDP Dispense As Written (DAW) code indicating whether the prescriber or member requested brand-name dispensing when a generic was available. Date the drug was dispensed to the member at the pharmacy. Represents the real-world service event date used for benefit period calculations, days supply tracking, and CMS Part D reporting.

The fee charged by the dispensing pharmacy for the professional service of dispensing the medication. Negotiated as part of PBM network contracts and reported separately from ingredient cost per CMS Part D requirements.

Coverage determination status for this drug on the applicable formulary at time of adjudication. Indicates whether the drug is covered, non-covered, excluded, subject to step therapy, or requires prior authorization (PA).

NCPDP DUR conflict code identifying the type of drug utilization issue detected during adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, duplicate therapy, high dose). Sequential fill number for this prescription across all fills, including original and all refills. Distinct from refill_number in that it counts total fills rather than refill sequence. Used for prescription lifecycle tracking.

Drug formulary tier assigned to this drug at the time of adjudication, determining the member's cost-sharing level (e.g., Tier 1 generic, Tier 2 preferred brand, Tier 3 non-preferred brand, Tier 4 specialty). Drives copay/coinsurance calculation.
Indicates whether the dispensed drug is a generic equivalent. Used for generic dispensing rate (GDR) reporting, formulary compliance analytics, and PBM performance measurement.

Total gross cost of the drug on this claim line, calculated as ingredient cost plus dispensing fee plus sales tax plus incentive amount. Used as the basis for CMS Part D gross drug cost reporting and risk corridor calculations.
Incentive or bonus amount paid to the pharmacy for performance-based or preferred network participation on this claim line. Included in gross drug cost calculations for CMS Part D reporting.

The drug ingredient cost component of the claim line, representing the cost of the drug itself exclusive of dispensing fee, sales tax, and other charges. Key input for MAC/AWP/WAC-based reimbursement calculations and compound drug cost analysis.
Sequential number identifying the position of this line within the parent pharmacy claim. Used to order compound ingredient lines and COB line splits. Starts at 1.

Current adjudication status of this pharmacy claim line. Drives payment processing, COB resolution, and audit reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid|denied|reversed|adjusted|pending|void — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]
Classifies the nature of this claim line: standard single-drug fill, compound ingredient line, COB line split, reversal, or adjustment. Drives compound cost analysis and COB workflows.
Indicates whether the member received a Low Income Subsidy (LIS) benefit on this claim line, resulting in reduced cost-sharing. Required for CMS Part D PDE reporting and LIS reconciliation.
Amount of manufacturer discount applied to this claim line under the CMS Part D Coverage Gap Discount Program. Counts toward the member's TrOOP and is reported on the PDE record.
11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product, strength, and package size dispensed on this claim line. Sourced from FDA NDC Directory. PHI when linked to a member.

Amount paid by another payer (e.g., secondary insurer, manufacturer coupon, government program) on this claim line as part of Coordination of Benefits (COB). Used to calculate the plan's net liability.

Total amount paid by the member/patient at point of sale, including copay, coinsurance, and deductible contributions. Used for Out-of-Pocket (OOP) accumulator tracking and Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) calculations.
Date this claim line was submitted to CMS as a Prescription Drug Event (PDE) record for Part D reconciliation and risk adjustment. Null for non-Part D claims. Critical for CMS compliance and audit.

Distribution channel through which the drug was dispensed: retail pharmacy, mail-order pharmacy, specialty pharmacy, long-term care (LTC) pharmacy, or home infusion. Drives network contract application, reimbursement rates, and utilization analytics.
Net amount paid by the health plan on this claim line after applying member cost-sharing, COB adjustments, and other payer amounts. Core financial metric for MLR and PMPM reporting.

Actual quantity of the drug dispensed (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, grams) as reported by the pharmacy. Used for quantity limit edits, compound ingredient cost allocation, and drug utilization review (DUR).
Indicates whether a quantity limit restriction was applied to this drug claim line during adjudication. When true, the dispensed quantity was subject to plan-defined quantity limit rules.

Refill sequence number for this prescription fill. Zero indicates the original fill; subsequent values indicate refill number. Used for refill-too-soon edits, adherence monitoring, and quantity limit enforcement.

Date and time when this pharmacy claim line was reversed, if applicable. Null for non-reversed lines. Used for financial reconciliation, audit trails, and identifying reversed claims in PMPM and MLR calculations.
State or local sales tax applied to this pharmacy claim line where applicable. Reported separately per CMS Part D PDE requirements. Zero or null in states with no drug sales tax.

Natural key or transaction identifier for this claim line as assigned by the originating PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for lineage tracing, reconciliation with source system, and EDI transaction matching.

Indicates whether the drug dispensed on this line is classified as a specialty drug, typically requiring special handling, storage, or administration and subject to specialty pharmacy channel requirements and PA rules.
Indicates whether this drug claim line was subject to a step therapy protocol requiring the member to try a preferred or lower-cost drug before coverage of this drug was approved.

Member's True Out-of-Pocket (TrOOP) cost contribution from this claim line, used to track progress toward the Part D catastrophic coverage threshold. Includes patient pay and applicable manufacturer discounts.
Unique surrogate identifier for each CMS Part D regulatory data submission record in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the primary key for this entity.

The CMS-assigned Plan Benefit Package identifier associated with this submission. Distinguishes specific benefit packages within a Part D contract for formulary, cost-sharing, and coverage gap reporting.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Part D submissions are a type of regulatory filing; linking to regulatory_submission records submission details and compliance tracking.

The number of records within the submission batch that CMS accepted and processed successfully. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Used to calculate acceptance rate and identify partial rejections.

Externally assigned batch identifier assigned by the CMS submission system (RAPS, EDPS, or CGDP portal) for the submission file. Used to correlate records across the submission lifecycle and match CMS acknowledgment responses.
The four-digit calendar year of the Medicare Part D benefit period to which this submission applies. Drives payment year reconciliation, risk adjustment settlement, and DIR reporting cycles with CMS.

business_glossary_term: Coverage Gap Discount Program (CGDP) Invoice Amount
description: The total dollar amount invoiced under the Coverage Gap Discount Program for this submission, applicable to CGDP submission types. Represents manufacturer discount obligations for brand-name drugs dispensed to Part D beneficiaries in the coverage gap.

The tracking or reference identifier assigned by the EDI clearinghouse (Availity or Change Healthcare) upon receipt of the submission file. Used to trace submission status through the clearinghouse to CMS.

The CMS-assigned Part D plan contract identifier (e.g., H1234 or S5678) under which this submission is filed. Identifies the health plan's Medicare Part D contract for regulatory reporting and payment purposes.
The CMS regional office code associated with the Part D contract under which this submission is filed. Used for regulatory routing and regional compliance reporting.

The acknowledgment or disposition code returned by CMS upon processing the submission batch. Maps to CMS-defined response code sets (e.g., RAPS response codes, EDPS acknowledgment codes). Used for automated error triage and compliance tracking.

business_glossary_term: CMS Response Receipt Date and Time
description: The timestamp when the CMS acknowledgment or response file was received by the health plan. Used to calculate CMS processing turnaround time and to trigger downstream reconciliation workflows.
Human-readable description of the CMS response code returned for this submission. Provides context for the acceptance, rejection, or error condition reported by CMS.
The timestamp when this Part D submission record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Serves as the record audit created timestamp for data lineage and ETL traceability.

The total Direct and Indirect Remuneration dollar amount reported in this submission, applicable to DIR submission types. Represents post-point-of-sale price concessions, rebates, and other remuneration from manufacturers and pharmacies that reduce net drug costs. Required for CMS Part D DIR reconciliation.

business_glossary_term: Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Interchange Control Number
description: The ISA13 interchange control number from the EDI transmission envelope, applicable to EDI-formatted submissions. Used to correlate submission files with EDI gateway acknowledgments (997/999) from the clearinghouse or CMS.

Structured or free-text description of errors returned by CMS for rejected or partially accepted records. May include CMS error codes, field-level rejection reasons, and record-level identifiers. Supports error remediation and resubmission workflows.

The number of Hierarchical Condition Category diagnosis records included in this submission batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Used to monitor HCC capture completeness and risk score optimization.

Indicates whether this submission is a resubmission of a previously rejected or corrected batch (True) or an original submission (False). Used to track correction cycles and measure first-pass acceptance rates.
Indicates whether the submission was transmitted to CMS on or before the regulatory deadline date (True) or after the deadline (False). Supports CMS compliance reporting and internal SLA monitoring.

The Medicare Part D line of business associated with this submission. MAPD = Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug plan; PDP = standalone Prescription Drug Plan; EGWP = Employer Group Waiver Plan; LIS = Low Income Subsidy. Drives regulatory reporting segmentation.

Free-text field for operational notes, manual override justifications, or compliance annotations associated with this submission record. Used by compliance and operations teams to document exception handling, CMS communications, or remediation actions.
For resubmissions, the batch_id of the original submission that this record corrects or replaces. Null for original submissions. Enables lineage tracing across submission correction cycles.

Identifies the source PBM system from which the submission data was extracted. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements. Values align to operational PBM platforms (RxClaim, Argus).

The number of Prescription Drug Event records included in this submission batch, applicable to PDE and EDPS submission types. PDE records are the foundational data element for CMS Part D payment and reconciliation.

The number of records within the submission batch that CMS has received but not yet finalized (e.g., held for additional review or in a processing queue). Supports real-time submission status monitoring.

The estimated revenue impact in dollars attributable to the risk adjustment data submitted in this batch, applicable to RAPS and EDPS submission types. Supports risk adjustment revenue integrity analysis and HCC-based payment reconciliation.
The date on which the submission was fully reconciled against the CMS response. Null if reconciliation is not yet complete. Used for SLA tracking and audit trail.

Indicates the reconciliation state of the submission against CMS response data. UNRECONCILED = CMS response not yet matched; IN_PROGRESS = reconciliation underway; RECONCILED = fully matched and closed; EXCEPTION = discrepancy identified requiring manual review.
The number of records within the submission batch that CMS rejected. Derived from the CMS acknowledgment response file. Drives error remediation workflows and resubmission prioritization.
The last calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Together with reporting_period_start_date, defines the data window for the submission batch.

The first calendar date of the reporting period covered by this Part D submission. Used to align submission data with the applicable benefit year, payment year, or reconciliation cycle as defined by CMS.

Sequential count of resubmission attempts for a given original batch. Value is 0 for original submissions, 1 for first resubmission, 2 for second, etc. Supports escalation workflows and CMS deadline compliance monitoring.

The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the submission batch was transmitted to CMS. Distinct from the record audit created timestamp. Used for timeliness compliance tracking against CMS filing deadlines.
The regulatory deadline date by which this submission must be received by CMS. Used to assess timeliness compliance and trigger escalation alerts for at-risk submissions.
The name of the file transmitted to CMS for this submission batch. Used for audit trail, resubmission tracking, and reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment files.

The electronic data interchange or file format used for the CMS submission. EDI_837 = ANSI X12 837 pharmacy claim transaction; FLAT_FILE = CMS-specified fixed-width flat file; XML = extensible markup language; CSV = comma-separated values.

Current workflow state of the Part D submission. PENDING = prepared but not yet transmitted; SUBMITTED = transmitted to CMS; ACCEPTED = CMS confirmed full acceptance; REJECTED = CMS rejected the batch; PARTIALLY_ACCEPTED = CMS accepted a subset of records; RECONCILED = submission reconciled against CMS response and closed.

Classifies the type of CMS Part D regulatory submission. RAPS = Risk Adjustment Processing System pharmacy encounter; EDPS = Encounter Data Processing System; DIR = Direct and Indirect Remuneration report; CGDP = Coverage Gap Discount Program invoice; PDE = Prescription Drug Event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: RAPS|EDPS|DIR|CGDP|PDE — promote to reference product if additional types are added]
The name of the organization or entity submitting the Part D data to CMS. May be the health plan, TPA, or PBM acting on behalf of the plan. Supports audit trail and accountability tracking.
The total number of records (encounters, claims, or line items) included in the submission batch as transmitted to CMS. Used as the control total for reconciliation against CMS acknowledgment.
The timestamp when this Part D submission record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Used to detect record changes, support incremental processing, and maintain audit trail integrity.
Unique surrogate identifier for each pharmacy claim record in the Silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the pharmacy_claim data product. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑Center Allocation: claim expense is allocated to the appropriate cost center for budgeting and variance analysis.

Surrogate reference to the pharmacy that dispensed the drug. Links to the provider/pharmacy master record. Distinguishes retail, mail-order, and specialty pharmacy channels. Used for network adequacy reporting and pharmacy audit trail requirements.

Surrogate reference to the NDC-level drug master record in the pharmacy domain. Links to the formulary drug master product which carries NDC, drug name, tier, and pricing (AWP/WAC/MAC). Central to formulary compliance and drug utilization review (DUR).

Foreign key linking to enrollment.enrollment_eligibility_span. Business justification: Required for claim adjudication to determine member's eligibility span at service date, supporting CMS reporting and accurate cost‑share calculations.

Foreign key linking to claim.header. Business justification: Required for integrated care reporting: links pharmacy claims to the corresponding medical claim header for episode‑of‑care analysis and coordinated benefits calculations.

Surrogate reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Links to the plan and benefit product. Determines formulary, cost-sharing rules, and CMS Part D contract identification.

Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Required for Claim Adjudication Posting process: each pharmacy claim expense must be posted to a GL ledger for financial reporting and regulatory audit.

Surrogate reference to the health plan member who is the beneficiary of this pharmacy claim. Links to the member master record in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT). Required for eligibility verification, coordination of benefits (COB), and CMS Part D risk adjustment (RAF/HCC).

Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Risk‑adjusted claim adjudication report requires each claim to reference the member's risk score to apply CMS risk adjustment factors.

Source system transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) at the time of adjudication. Used for claim traceability, PBM reconciliation, EDI 835 remittance matching, and pharmacy audit trail. Enables lineage back to the operational system of record.

Foreign key linking to credential.credential_record. Business justification: Claim adjudication requires verifying prescriber credentials per CMS regulations; linking claim to credential_record enables automated credential status checks.

Surrogate reference to the prior authorization record that approved dispensing of this drug, if applicable. Null when no PA was required. Links to the utilization management PA product. Required for specialty drug claims and step therapy override tracking.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Claim Processing Audit Report to track which employee adjudicated each pharmacy claim.

Foreign key linking to utilization.um_case. Business justification: Claims are associated with utilization‑management cases for case‑level cost analysis and outcome tracking, required by case management dashboards.

Date and time the pharmacy claim was adjudicated (approved or rejected) by the PBM system. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing event). Used for claims processing SLA measurement, 835 remittance matching, and audit trail compliance.

Code indicating the pricing benchmark used to determine the ingredient cost for this claim. AWP = Average Wholesale Price; WAC = Wholesale Acquisition Cost; MAC = Maximum Allowable Cost; usual_and_customary = pharmacy's standard retail price; negotiated = contracted rate; 340B = 340B program pricing; other = alternative basis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: awp|wac|mac|usual_and_customary|negotiated|340b|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Externally-known, human-readable claim reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) at adjudication. Used for member communications, Explanation of Benefits (EOB), and 835 remittance reconciliation. Maps to NCPDP D.0 transaction reference number.

Current adjudication lifecycle status of the pharmacy claim as determined by the PBM system. 'paid' = claim adjudicated and payment issued; 'rejected' = claim denied with reject code; 'reversed' = previously paid claim reversed; 'adjusted' = claim reprocessed with changed amounts; 'pending' = awaiting adjudication; 'void' = cancelled before processing.

Indicates whether coordination of benefits (COB) was applied on this claim (True = COB applied, another payer was involved). Triggers COB segment processing and other_payer_amount population. Used for COB reconciliation and CMS Part D TrOOP calculations.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Claim adjudication uses PBM contract rates, step‑therapy and rebate rules; linking claim to provider_contract enables accurate pricing and compliance reporting.

Timestamp when this pharmacy claim record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and HIPAA record retention requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

NCPDP Dispense As Written (DAW) code indicating whether a brand drug was dispensed when a generic was available, and the reason. 0 = no product selection indicated; 1 = substitution not allowed by prescriber; 2 = substitution allowed, patient requested brand; 3 = substitution allowed, pharmacist selected brand; 4 = substitution allowed, generic not in stock; 5 = substitution allowed, brand dispensed as generic. Used for generic substitution rate analytics and formulary compliance.

Number of days of medication supply dispensed on this claim as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for refill-too-soon edits, medication adherence (MPR/PDC) calculations, HEDIS pharmacy measures, and CMS Part D days supply validation.

Dollar amount of this pharmacy claim applied toward the member's pharmacy or combined medical/pharmacy deductible. Relevant for HDHP/HSA plans and CMS Part D deductible phase tracking. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.

Fee charged by the dispensing pharmacy for the act of dispensing the prescription, separate from ingredient cost. Negotiated as part of the PBM network contract. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and total claim cost analysis.

10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the dispensing pharmacy as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability and NCPDP D.0 compliance. Used for pharmacy network validation, audit trail, and CMS Part D encounter data.

NCPDP standard code indicating the outcome of the prospective drug utilization review (DUR) edit performed during adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, duplicate therapy, dose check). The date the prescription was dispensed by the pharmacy to the member. This is the principal real-world business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category). Used for days supply calculations, refill-too-soon edits, and CMS Part D encounter data date of service reporting.

Formulary tier assigned to the dispensed drug at the time of adjudication. Determines member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) and plan benefit design. Tier 1 = preferred generic; Tier 2 = generic; Tier 3 = preferred brand; Tier 4 = non-preferred brand; Tier 5 = specialty; non_formulary = not on formulary.

The cost of the drug ingredient(s) as billed by the dispensing pharmacy, before dispensing fee and other adjustments. Basis may be AWP, WAC, MAC, or negotiated rate. Core component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET for pharmacy claim financials. Used for PBM contract reconciliation and MLR calculation.

Indicates whether this pharmacy claim was dispensed under the 340B Drug Pricing Program (True = 340B claim). Used for 340B duplicate discount prevention, HRSA compliance reporting, and separation of 340B and non-340B claims in financial reconciliation.

Indicates whether this claim is for a compounded drug product containing multiple ingredients (True = compound claim). Compound claims require line-level detail in the pharmacy_claim_line product. Used for compound drug utilization review and cost management.

Line of business under which this pharmacy claim was adjudicated. Drives regulatory reporting requirements, formulary rules, and cost-sharing structures. 'commercial' = employer-sponsored or individual; 'medicare_part_d' = CMS Medicare Part D; 'medicaid' = state Medicaid; 'exchange' = ACA marketplace QHP; 'cobra' = COBRA continuation coverage.

Percentage-based cost-sharing amount paid by the member for this pharmacy claim, calculated as a percentage of the allowed cost. Applies when the benefit design uses coinsurance rather than fixed copay. Contributes to OOP/MOOP accumulation.

Fixed dollar amount paid by the member at point of sale as their cost-sharing obligation for this pharmacy claim. Determined by formulary tier and benefit design. Contributes to member out-of-pocket (OOP) and maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) accumulation.

11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product dispensed, including labeler, product, and package segment. Denormalized from the drug master for claim-level auditability and CMS Part D encounter data submission (EDPS). Required for 340B duplicate discount prevention and drug utilization review (DUR).

Claim number of the original pharmacy claim that this record adjusts or reverses. Populated only for adjusted or reversed claims; null for original claims. Enables claim adjustment chain tracing for financial reconciliation, audit, and CMS Part D encounter data correction submissions.

Amount paid by another payer (secondary insurer, Medicare, Medicaid, manufacturer coupon) under coordination of benefits (COB). Used for COB reconciliation, 340B duplicate discount prevention, and CMS Part D TrOOP (True Out-of-Pocket) calculations.

Channel through which the drug was dispensed. Drives cost-sharing differentials, formulary tier rules, and network adequacy reporting. 'retail' = community pharmacy; 'mail_order' = 90-day mail service; 'specialty' = specialty pharmacy for high-cost/complex drugs; 'long_term_care' = SNF/LTC pharmacy; 'home_infusion' = infusion therapy at home.

Total amount paid by the health plan for this pharmacy claim after applying member cost-sharing (copay, coinsurance, deductible) and COB adjustments. Net total component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET. Used for 835 remittance, MLR reporting, and CMS Part D encounter data.

10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing provider as submitted on the pharmacy claim. Denormalized for claim-level auditability, NCPDP D.0 compliance, and CMS Part D encounter data submission. Used for prescriber fraud/waste/abuse (FWA) analytics.

Date the prescription was written by the prescriber. Used to validate prescription age, enforce days-supply-from-written-date rules, and support medication adherence analytics. Distinct from fill_date (dispensing date).

Actual quantity of the drug dispensed (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, grams) as reported by the dispensing pharmacy. Used for quantity limit (QL) edits, drug utilization review (DUR), and CMS Part D encounter data submission.

NCPDP standard unit of measure code for the quantity dispensed. EA = each (tablets/capsules); ML = milliliters; GM = grams; UN = units; F2 = international units; ME = milliequivalents. Required for accurate quantity limit (QL) edit enforcement and CMS Part D encounter data.

Refill sequence number for this dispensing event. Zero (0) indicates the original fill; subsequent values indicate refill number. Used for refill-too-soon edits, medication adherence tracking, and prescription lifecycle management.

NCPDP standard reject code returned by the PBM system when a claim is rejected or denied. Null for paid claims. Used for pharmacy operations, member appeals, and drug utilization review (DUR) outcome tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product — NCPDP reject codes exceed 6 values]

Sales tax amount applied to the pharmacy claim where applicable by state law. Included in total claim cost and reported separately for financial reconciliation and statutory accounting (SAP) purposes.

Timestamp when this pharmacy claim record was last modified in the Silver layer, such as after a claim adjustment, reversal, or COB update. Supports change data capture (CDC) and audit trail requirements. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.

Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which the member is enrolled at the time of the DUR alert. Used to apply plan-specific DUR rules, formulary restrictions, and CMS Part D contract reporting.
Unique identifier of the health plan member for whom the DUR alert was triggered. Used to associate the alert with the member's medication history and clinical profile.
Unique transaction identifier assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) to the adjudication transaction that generated this DUR alert. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit trail.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: DUR alerts are reviewed by a clinical reviewer employee; audit of DUR decisions needs this link.

Final adjudication outcome of the pharmacy claim after the DUR alert was processed. Indicates whether the claim was paid, rejected due to the alert, overridden by the pharmacist or plan, pending review, or reversed.
Date and time when the DUR alert was generated by the PBM adjudication engine (RxClaim or Argus) during real-time claim processing. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this record.

Current lifecycle status of the DUR alert record. 'active'=alert is open and requires action, 'resolved'=alert addressed and closed, 'overridden'=pharmacist or plan overrode the alert, 'suppressed'=alert suppressed per plan rules, 'escalated'=referred for clinical review.

NCPDP standardized code identifying the category of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert. Common values: DD=Drug-Drug Interaction, DC=Drug-Disease Contraindication, TD=Therapeutic Duplication, PA=Drug-Allergy, LD=Low Dose, HD=High Dose, MN=Drug-Age, CS=Drug-Gender, AD=Additive Toxicity, RF=Early Refill. Human-readable description of the DUR alert type (e.g., 'Drug-Drug Interaction', 'Therapeutic Duplication', 'High Dose Alert', 'Early Refill'). Complements the alert_type_code for reporting and member communications.

Numeric code from the clinical drug database (First DataBank or Medi-Span) indicating the clinical significance of the drug interaction or contraindication. Ranges from 1 (most severe) to 5 (least severe). Supplements the NCPDP severity_level_code with database-specific grading.

Timestamp when the DUR alert record was first created and persisted in the data platform (Databricks Silver Layer). Represents the audit trail creation time, distinct from the business event timestamp (alert_generated_timestamp).
Number of days elapsed since the member's last fill of the same or therapeutically equivalent drug. Key metric for early refill DUR alert generation and medication adherence analysis.
Number of days of medication supply dispensed with the prescription. Used in early refill DUR calculations, quantity limit evaluations, and high-dose alert assessments.
Date on which the drug was dispensed at the pharmacy. Used to correlate the DUR alert with the dispensing event and for retrospective DUR analysis and CMS Part D reporting.
10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacy that dispensed the drug and at which the DUR alert was generated during point-of-sale adjudication.

Identifies the clinical drug knowledge database used to generate the DUR alert (e.g., First DataBank (FDB), Medi-Span, Micromedex, Multum, or custom plan rules). Important for audit, reproducibility, and database version tracking.

Version or effective date of the clinical drug knowledge database (e.g., FDB or Medi-Span) used at the time the DUR alert was generated. Required for audit trail and regulatory reproducibility of DUR alert decisions.
Brand or generic name of the primary drug (identified by drug_ndc) that triggered the DUR alert. Denormalized for reporting and pharmacist display without requiring a drug master join.
11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) that triggered the DUR alert. Primary drug identifier used in pharmacy adjudication.

Indicates whether the DUR alert was generated as part of a prospective (point-of-sale, real-time), retrospective (post-dispensing, claims analysis), or concurrent (during hospitalization or ongoing therapy) DUR program.
