description: Version of the ICD coding system used for the icd_diagnosis_code on this DUR alert record (e.g., ICD-10-CM). Required for accurate clinical coding interpretation and regulatory reporting.

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Diagnosis Code
description: ICD-10-CM diagnosis code associated with the drug-disease contraindication or drug-condition DUR alert. Identifies the member's condition that conflicts with the dispensed drug. Populated for DC (drug-disease) alert types.

Brand or generic name of the interacting drug involved in a drug-drug interaction or therapeutic duplication alert. Provides human-readable context for pharmacist review and DUR reporting without requiring a drug master lookup.

11-digit NDC of the second drug involved in a drug-drug interaction (DDI) or therapeutic duplication DUR alert. Populated when alert_type_code indicates a drug-drug conflict. Null for non-interaction alert types.

business_glossary_term: NCPDP Drug Utilization Review (DUR) Conflict Code
description: Two-character NCPDP standardized DUR conflict code transmitted in the pharmacy claim transaction (Field 439-E4). Identifies the specific clinical conflict detected (e.g., 'DD' for drug-drug interaction). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.

Two-digit NCPDP reject code returned when the DUR alert results in a claim rejection (e.g., '88'=DUR Reject Error). Populated when adjudication_outcome is 'rejected'. Used for claim denial tracking and appeals.

Two-character NCPDP code submitted by the pharmacist to override a DUR alert and allow dispensing (e.g., 'OO'=Other Override, 'OP'=Prescriber Override, 'OR'=Pharmacist Override). Populated when adjudication_outcome is 'overridden'.

Free-text clinical justification or pharmacist notes entered when overriding a DUR alert. Documents the clinical rationale for proceeding with dispensing despite the alert. Supports audit and retrospective DUR review.
Reference number of the Prior Authorization (PA) request or approval associated with this DUR alert. Populated when pa_required_flag is True and a PA has been submitted or approved.

Indicates whether the DUR alert outcome requires a Prior Authorization (PA) to be obtained before the drug can be dispensed. True when the alert triggers a PA requirement per plan formulary or step therapy rules.
Quantity of the drug as written on the original prescription by the prescriber. Compared against quantity_dispensed and plan quantity limits in DUR alert evaluation.
10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or clinician who ordered the drug associated with the DUR alert. Used for prescriber-level DUR reporting and outreach.
Date the prescription was originally written by the prescriber. Used in early refill DUR alerts to calculate days since prescription was issued and validate refill timing.

Two-character NCPDP code (Field 440-E5) indicating the professional service performed by the pharmacist in response to the DUR alert (e.g., 'AS'=Patient Assessment, 'CC'=Coordination of Care, 'DE'=Dosing Evaluation, 'PM'=Patient Medication Education). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.
Quantity of the drug dispensed (in units as defined by the drug's billing unit of measure). Used in high-dose and quantity limit DUR alert evaluations.
Indicates whether the DUR alert was triggered by a quantity limit edit, where the dispensed or prescribed quantity exceeds the plan's maximum allowable quantity for the drug.

Two-character NCPDP code (Field 439-E4 context) providing the clinical reason for the DUR service intervention. Captures the specific clinical basis for the alert and pharmacist action. Used in prospective DUR programs.

Sequential refill number for the prescription (0=original fill, 1=first refill, etc.). Used in early refill DUR alert logic to determine if the member is refilling too soon relative to the previous fill's days supply.

Two-character NCPDP code (Field 441-E6) indicating the outcome of the pharmacist's intervention in response to the DUR alert (e.g., 'FA'=Filled As Is, 'FN'=Filled with Prescriber Approval, 'FC'=Filled, Calling Prescriber, 'FD'=Filled, Directions Clarified). Required for CMS Part D DUR reporting.

Coded severity level of the DUR alert indicating clinical significance. Typically: 1=Contraindicated/Severe, 2=Major, 3=Moderate, 4=Minor, 5=Informational. Drives pharmacist override requirements and adjudication workflow.
Plain-language description of the DUR alert severity level (e.g., 'Contraindicated', 'Major', 'Moderate', 'Minor', 'Informational'). Used in pharmacist-facing displays and DUR reporting dashboards.

Indicates whether the DUR alert is related to a step therapy protocol violation, where the member has not tried a required first-line drug before the prescribed drug. Supports step therapy enforcement and CMS Part D reporting.

Timestamp when the DUR alert record was last modified in the data platform. Tracks updates such as status changes, override entries, or adjudication outcome revisions for audit and data lineage purposes.
Reference to the Part D prescription drug plan (PDP) or Medicare Advantage Prescription Drug (MA-PD) plan under which the member is enrolled and MTM eligibility is determined.
Reference to the health plan member who received the MTM service. Links to the member master record in the core administration platform (Facets/QNXT).
Identifier for the specific MTM program offering under which this service was delivered. A plan sponsor may operate multiple MTM programs targeting different chronic condition cohorts.

Number of qualifying chronic conditions the member has at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of chronic conditions (typically 2-3 depending on plan criteria). Used for eligibility validation and population stratification.

Date on which the annual Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) was completed for the member. CMS requires at least one CMR per calendar year for all enrolled MTM beneficiaries. Directly drives the CMS STAR Ratings MTM CMR Completion Rate measure.

CMS Part D reporting period to which this MTM service record is attributed (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-ANNUAL). Used for CMS Part D MTM program reporting submissions and STAR ratings measurement year alignment.

Total number of outreach attempts made to the member to schedule or deliver the MTM service. CMS requires documentation of good-faith efforts to reach enrolled members. Used for compliance audits and outreach effectiveness analytics.

Timestamp when this MTM service record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Date on which the member was disenrolled or opted out of the MTM program. Null if the member remains enrolled. Used to track program retention and calculate MTM completion rates.

Primary category of drug therapy problem identified during the MTM service. Common types include: unnecessary drug therapy, needs additional drug therapy, ineffective drug, dosage too low, dosage too high, adverse drug reaction, non-adherence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNNECESSARY_DRUG|NEEDS_ADDITIONAL_DRUG|INEFFECTIVE_DRUG|DOSE_TOO_LOW|DOSE_TOO_HIGH|ADVERSE_REACTION|NON_ADHERENCE|NONE — promote to reference product]

NCPDP-standard Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code documenting the result of the pharmacist's clinical intervention during the MTM service (e.g., 1A = filled as is, 1B = filled with prescriber approval, 1C = not filled). Sourced from RxClaim or Argus PBM system DUR transaction records.

Date on which the member was determined to be eligible for the MTM program based on CMS Part D criteria (multiple chronic conditions, multiple Part D drugs, likely to incur annual costs above the CMS threshold).
Date on which the eligible member was enrolled into the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment of all eligible beneficiaries with opt-out capability.

business_glossary_term: Estimated Annual Part D Drug Cost
description: Estimated annual cost of Part D covered drugs for the member at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS requires members likely to incur annual drug costs above the established threshold to be enrolled. Expressed in USD.

Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up contact or service following the MTM encounter. Null if no follow-up is required or scheduled. Used to track closure of open MAP items and clinical recommendations.

Indicates whether a follow-up contact or service is required after the current MTM encounter based on clinical findings or unresolved MAP items. True = follow-up required; False = no follow-up needed.

Current status of the follow-up action required after the MTM service. PENDING = follow-up scheduled but not yet completed; COMPLETED = follow-up successfully conducted; DECLINED = member declined follow-up; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted for follow-up; NOT_REQUIRED = no follow-up needed.

Total number of clinical interventions or recommendations made during the MTM service encounter (e.g., drug therapy problem resolutions, adherence recommendations, prescriber referrals). Used for quality reporting and pharmacist productivity analytics.
Date of the most recent outreach attempt made to the member for MTM scheduling or delivery. Used alongside contact_attempt_count to document good-faith outreach efforts for CMS compliance.

Number of action items documented in the Medication Action Plan (MAP) provided to the member. Each MAP item represents a specific recommendation or goal agreed upon between the pharmacist and the member.

Indicates whether a written Medication Action Plan (MAP) was provided to the member following the CMR. CMS requires a standardized MAP to be delivered to the member and their prescriber(s) after each CMR. True = MAP provided; False = MAP not yet provided.

Indicates whether the member provided consent to participate in the MTM service and for sharing of clinical findings with their prescriber(s). True = consent obtained; False = consent not obtained or pending. Required for HIPAA compliance when sharing PHI with prescribers.

Indicates whether the member has opted out of the MTM program. CMS requires auto-enrollment with opt-out capability. True = member has opted out; False = member remains enrolled. When True, disenrollment_date should be populated.
Reason code for the member's opt-out or disenrollment from the MTM program. Null if the member has not opted out. Used for program retention analysis and CMS reporting.

Number of Part D covered drugs the member is taking at the time of MTM eligibility determination. CMS Part D MTM eligibility requires a minimum number of Part D drugs (typically 8 or more). Used for eligibility validation.
Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) used to cross-reference the MTM record with pharmacy claims and formulary data.

Indicates whether a Personal Medication List (PML) was provided to the member as part of the CMR standardized format. CMS requires both a MAP and a PML to be delivered following each CMR. True = PML provided; False = PML not provided.

10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the pharmacist or clinical pharmacist who delivered the MTM service, including Comprehensive Medication Review (CMR) or Targeted Medication Review (TMR). Required for CMS Part D MTM reporting.

Free-text clinical notes documenting the pharmacist's recommendations, findings, and interventions during the MTM service encounter. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI) and must be handled in accordance with HIPAA Privacy Rule requirements.
Date on which the prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings and MAP items. Null if notification has not yet been sent. Used for compliance tracking against CMS timelines.

Indicates whether the member's prescriber(s) were notified of the MTM service findings, recommendations, or MAP items. CMS requires that the MAP and relevant clinical findings be shared with the prescriber. True = prescriber notified; False = notification pending or not completed.

Calendar date on which the MTM service (CMR, TMR, or follow-up) was delivered to the member. This is the principal real-world event date used for CMS Part D MTM reporting and STAR ratings quality measure calculations.

Mode through which the MTM service was delivered to the member. CMS requires CMRs to be offered as interactive, person-to-person consultations (in-person or telehealth). Supports channel-level analytics and compliance verification.

Duration of the MTM service encounter in minutes. Used for pharmacist productivity reporting, billing reconciliation with PBM, and quality analytics. CMR encounters typically range from 30 to 60 minutes.

Current lifecycle status of the MTM service record. SCHEDULED = appointment set; IN_PROGRESS = service initiated; COMPLETED = service fully delivered and documented; DECLINED = member declined participation; UNREACHABLE = member could not be contacted; CANCELLED = service cancelled prior to delivery.

Classification of the MTM service delivered. CMR = Comprehensive Medication Review (annual face-to-face or telehealth); TMR = Targeted Medication Review (quarterly or as needed); FOLLOW_UP = follow-up contact on prior CMR/TMR; REFERRAL = referral to prescriber or specialist; OTHER = other MTM-related service.

Code identifying the operational source system from which this MTM service record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across PBM (RxClaim/Argus) and Care Management (Casenet TruCare/Altruista) platforms.

Native record identifier from the originating source system (RxClaim, Argus, Casenet TruCare, or Altruista) for this MTM service record. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer lakehouse.

Indicates whether this MTM service record is eligible to be counted toward the CMS STAR Ratings MTM Completion Rate for Comprehensive Medication Reviews (CMR) quality measure. True = eligible for STAR measure numerator/denominator; False = excluded from STAR measure calculation.

Date on which the most recent Targeted Medication Review (TMR) was conducted. TMRs are performed at least quarterly for enrolled members who have not yet received a CMR or as clinically indicated. Null if no TMR has been performed.

Timestamp when this MTM service record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, incremental processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Identifier of the clinical reviewer (pharmacist, physician, or nurse) who made the prior authorization decision in the Utilization Management system (InterQual/MCG). Required for audit trail and URAC UM accreditation.
Identifier of the formulary associated with the member's plan at the time of the prior authorization request. Determines drug tier, coverage rules, and applicable PA criteria.
Identifier of the health plan or benefit plan under which the prior authorization is being evaluated. Determines applicable formulary, tier rules, and PA criteria.

Foreign key linking to risk.member_risk_score. Business justification: Prior‑authorization workflow uses the member's risk score to assess clinical necessity and cost containment, mandated by internal utilization management policies.
Identifier of the health plan member for whom the prior authorization is requested. Links to the member master record in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
Reference to the original prior authorization record when this record represents a renewal, appeal, or modification. Enables tracking of the full PA lifecycle chain. Null for initial requests.

Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_decision. Business justification: Needed for compliance reporting that ties each prior authorization to the final decision, supporting regulatory PA decision audit.

Foreign key linking to utilization.pa_request. Business justification: Required for PA audit report linking each prior authorization record to its originating request, enabling traceability of request‑to‑authorization lifecycle.
Indicates whether this prior authorization record is associated with a member or prescriber appeal of a prior denial. True = this PA is an appeal case.
Number of days of drug therapy authorized under this prior authorization. Used by the PBM to enforce dispensing limits and by CMS Part D for compliance reporting.
Total quantity of the drug authorized for dispensing under this prior authorization (e.g., number of tablets, milliliters, units). Enforced at point-of-sale by the PBM adjudication system.
Number of refills authorized under this prior authorization in addition to the initial fill. Enforced by the PBM adjudication system at point-of-sale.

Code identifying the specific clinical criteria set applied during the prior authorization review, sourced from the Utilization Management system (InterQual or MCG). Used for audit, appeals, and regulatory reporting.

Version of the clinical criteria set (InterQual or MCG) applied at the time of the prior authorization decision. Required for audit trails and appeals to ensure the correct criteria version is referenced.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Reportable Indicator
description: Indicates whether this prior authorization record is subject to CMS Part D PA reporting requirements. True = must be included in CMS Part D PA reporting submissions.
Timestamp when this prior authorization record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and Silver Layer processing compliance.

Indicates whether the submitted clinical information met the applicable clinical criteria (InterQual/MCG) for the requested drug. True = criteria met (supports approval); False = criteria not met (supports denial).

Exact date and time the prior authorization decision (approval, denial, or pend) was rendered by the clinical reviewer or automated adjudication engine. Used to calculate turnaround time against CMS and URAC standards.

Standardized code indicating the reason for denial of the prior authorization request (e.g., not medically necessary, step therapy not completed, quantity limit exceeded, non-covered drug). Populated only when pa_status = denied. Used for member appeals and CMS Part D reporting.

Free-text description of the denial reason, providing clinical or administrative context beyond the denial reason code. Included in member and prescriber denial notices per CMS and state DOI requirements.

Pharmacy dispensing channel authorized for this prior authorization (retail, mail-order, specialty pharmacy, long-term care, or home infusion). Determines which pharmacy network is eligible to dispense.
11-digit National Drug Code (NDC) identifying the specific drug product, strength, and package size for which prior authorization is requested. Sourced from the PBM drug master (RxClaim/Argus).
Formulary tier of the requested drug at the time of the prior authorization request. Determines cost-sharing obligations and PA criteria applied.

NCPDP-standardized Drug Utilization Review (DUR) outcome code resulting from prospective DUR screening performed during PA adjudication (e.g., drug-drug interaction, drug-disease contraindication, duplicate therapy). Sourced from PBM system (RxClaim/Argus).
Date on which the prior authorization expires and is no longer valid for dispensing. After this date, a new PA request is required. Nullable for open-ended authorizations.
Date from which the approved prior authorization is valid for dispensing. Pharmacies and PBM adjudication systems use this date to confirm dispensing eligibility.

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Primary Diagnosis Code
description: Primary ICD-10-CM diagnosis code submitted by the prescriber to support the clinical necessity of the requested drug. Required for clinical criteria evaluation and CMS Part D PA reporting. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).

Line of Business (LOB) under which the member's coverage falls, determining applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and PA criteria (e.g., Commercial, Medicare Part D, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace).

Free-text clinical notes entered by the reviewing clinician during the prior authorization evaluation, capturing clinical rationale, additional context, or instructions for the prescriber or pharmacy. Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).

Externally-known, human-readable prior authorization reference number assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus) or Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG). Used on Explanation of Benefits (EOB) and communicated to prescribers and pharmacies.

Current lifecycle status of the prior authorization request. Drives dispensing eligibility at the pharmacy and CMS Part D PA reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: approved|denied|pending|cancelled|expired|appealed — promote to reference product if additional statuses are needed]

Category of prior authorization criteria applied, indicating the clinical or formulary reason the drug requires PA review (e.g., specialty drug, non-preferred tier, step therapy requirement, quantity limit, age restriction, off-label use). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty|non_preferred|step_therapy|quantity_limit|age_restriction|off_label — promote to reference product]

10-digit National Provider Identifier (NPI) of the prescribing physician or authorized prescriber requesting the prior authorization. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource) and validated against NPPES.
Indicates whether a quantity limit restriction was a factor in the prior authorization evaluation. True = quantity limit rules were applied during adjudication.

Exact date and time the prior authorization request was received by the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual/MCG) or PBM system (RxClaim/Argus). Used for precise turnaround time calculation and regulatory compliance.
Calendar date on which the prior authorization request was submitted by the prescriber or pharmacy. Used for turnaround time measurement and CMS Part D compliance reporting.
Classification of the prior authorization request indicating whether it is a new request, renewal of an existing authorization, appeal of a denial, expedited urgent request, or retrospective review.

Level of clinical review applied to the prior authorization request, indicating whether it was reviewed by a pharmacist, physician, peer-to-peer consultation, or medical director. Escalation level affects turnaround time standards.

business_glossary_term: Secondary International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Diagnosis Codes
description: Pipe-delimited list of secondary ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes submitted to support clinical necessity. Stored as a delimited string per Silver Layer conventions (no ARRAY type). Contains Protected Health Information (PHI).

Native prior authorization identifier from the originating operational system (RxClaim, Argus, InterQual, or MCG). Preserved for reconciliation, audit, and cross-system traceability in the Silver Layer.

Indicates whether the requested drug is classified as a specialty drug requiring specialty pharmacy dispensing and enhanced clinical review. Specialty drugs typically have higher cost-sharing and stricter PA criteria.

Indicates whether the member has documented evidence of completing the required step therapy (tried and failed prerequisite drugs) as required by the formulary PA criteria. Populated when step_therapy_required = True.

Indicates whether the prior authorization requires the member to have tried and failed one or more preferred or lower-tier drugs (step therapy) before the requested drug is authorized. True = step therapy applies.

Timestamp when this prior authorization record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform, reflecting status changes, decision updates, or data corrections. Used for change data capture and audit.
Unique surrogate identifier for the specialty drug program record. Primary key for this entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
Reference to the formulary under which this specialty drug program operates. Links the program to the applicable drug tier structure and coverage rules managed in the PBM system.
Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this specialty drug program is offered. Links to the plan and benefit domain for coverage determination and member eligibility.
Reference to the PBM contract governing the administration of this specialty drug program, including pricing terms (MAC, AWP, WAC), dispensing fees, and performance guarantees.
Source system identifier for this program as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for reconciliation between the lakehouse and the operational PBM system.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Specialty drug programs are overseen by a program manager employee; required for program governance and reporting.

Indicates whether the Copay Accumulator Adjustment Program (CAAP) applies to this specialty drug program. When true, manufacturer copay assistance payments do NOT count toward the member's deductible or Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP). Critical for CMS Part D compliance.

Contracted discount percentage applied to the Average Wholesale Price (AWP) for specialty drugs dispensed under this program. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1800 = 18% discount). Governs ingredient cost reimbursement to specialty pharmacies.

Indicates whether drugs in this program require active clinical management, including disease management, case management, or care coordination through the Care Management Platform (Casenet TruCare or Altruista).

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Specialty Tier Flag
description: Indicates whether this program is reportable to CMS under the Part D specialty tier requirements. Drives inclusion in CMS Part D formulary submissions and Star Ratings (STAR) calculations.

Indicates whether drugs in this program require temperature-controlled cold chain handling and storage during dispensing and shipping. Drives specialty pharmacy network eligibility and logistics requirements.

Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (copay card) is available for drugs in this specialty program. Triggers copay accumulator adjustment program (CAAP) evaluation logic.
Maximum dollar amount of manufacturer copay assistance benefit available to a member per program year under this specialty drug program. Used in accumulator adjustment calculations and OOP tracking.

Name of the manufacturer-sponsored copay assistance program (e.g., copay card program) associated with this specialty drug program. Used for affordability program tracking and OIG compliance reporting.
Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements under HIPAA and CMS.
Contracted dispensing fee paid to specialty pharmacies per prescription dispensed under this program. Expressed in USD. Component of total pharmacy reimbursement alongside ingredient cost.

Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) applied to claims under this specialty program. Prospective DUR occurs at point-of-sale; retrospective DUR occurs post-adjudication for quality and safety analysis.
Date on which the specialty drug program becomes active and eligible for member enrollment and claim adjudication. Aligns with plan year or contract effective dates.

Indicates whether independent charitable foundation grants are available to assist members with cost-sharing obligations for drugs in this specialty program. Foundation grants may or may not count toward OOP accumulators depending on CMS guidance.

Indicates whether members must enroll in a manufacturer-sponsored hub services program to access drugs under this specialty program. Hub services may include patient support, adherence programs, and financial assistance coordination.

business_glossary_term: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) Condition Codes
description: Comma-delimited list of ICD-10-CM diagnosis codes representing the covered clinical conditions for which drugs in this program are indicated. Used for prior authorization (PA) criteria and medical necessity determination.
Timestamp when the specialty drug program record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.
Indicates whether the drugs in this program are classified as Limited Distribution Drugs (LDD), available only through a restricted network of specialty pharmacies designated by the manufacturer.
The insurance line of business (LOB) under which this specialty drug program operates. Determines applicable regulatory requirements, formulary rules, and CMS reporting obligations.
Indicates whether drugs in this program may be dispensed via mail-order pharmacy channels in addition to retail specialty pharmacies.
Maximum number of days supply permitted per dispensing event for specialty drugs under this program. Typically 30 days for specialty drugs per CMS Part D guidance. Enforced at PBM adjudication.

Indicates whether third-party assistance payments (copay cards, foundation grants) applied to this program count toward the member's Out-of-Pocket (OOP) and Maximum Out-of-Pocket (MOOP) accumulators. Governs accumulator adjustment compliance.
Indicates whether a manufacturer-sponsored Patient Assistance Program (PAP) is available for uninsured or underinsured members enrolled in this specialty drug program.

Indicates whether Prior Authorization (PA) is required for all drugs covered under this specialty program before dispensing. Drives integration with the Utilization Management (UM) system (InterQual or MCG).

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the specialty drug program within the Pharmacy Benefit Management (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used in EDI transactions and CMS Part D reporting.

Detailed narrative description of the specialty drug program, including clinical rationale, covered conditions, and program objectives. Used in plan documents, Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC), and member communications.
Human-readable name of the specialty drug program (e.g., 'Oncology Specialty Tier Program', 'Rheumatology Hub Services Program'). Used in member-facing communications and plan documents.
Current lifecycle state of the specialty drug program. Controls whether the program is available for member enrollment and claim adjudication.

Classification of the specialty drug program by its primary operational function. Drives downstream adjudication logic and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: specialty_tier|hub_services|copay_assistance|patient_assistance|rems|cold_chain|limited_distribution — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether quantity limit (QL) restrictions apply to drugs dispensed under this specialty program. Quantity limits are enforced at the PBM adjudication level.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Required Flag
description: Indicates whether the specialty drugs in this program are subject to an FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS). When true, dispensing requires REMS-certified pharmacy and prescriber enrollment.
Name of the specialty drug list (SDL) associated with this program, identifying the set of National Drug Codes (NDCs) covered under the program. Managed in the PBM formulary system.
Name of the specialty pharmacy network designated to dispense drugs under this program. Defines the restricted network of pharmacies eligible to fill specialty prescriptions.
Formulary tier designation assigned to specialty drugs in this program. Determines member cost-sharing obligations and CMS Part D specialty tier reporting requirements.

Indicates whether step therapy protocols are enforced for drugs in this program, requiring members to try and fail on preferred therapies before accessing specialty drugs. Governed by state step therapy laws and CMS guidance.
Date on which the specialty drug program is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended program with no scheduled end. Used for plan year transitions and CMS Part D reporting.

Primary therapeutic or disease category associated with the specialty drugs in this program (e.g., Oncology, Rheumatology, Multiple Sclerosis, HIV/AIDS). Used for clinical reporting, HEDIS measures, and population health analytics.
Contracted discount percentage applied to the Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for specialty drugs in this program. Used as an alternative pricing basis to AWP in PBM contract negotiations.

Unique surrogate identifier for each drug master record in the lakehouse Silver layer. Serves as the primary key for all downstream joins across formulary, claims, DUR, rebate contracting, and PBM adjudication.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Data governance requires tracking which employee entered or updated drug master records.

Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor. Business justification: Required for drug rebate and pricing management: linking each drug to its manufacturer/vendor enables accurate rebate calculations, pricing negotiations, and CMS reporting.

The WHO Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification code at the 5th level, identifying the drug's anatomical main group, therapeutic subgroup, pharmacological subgroup, chemical subgroup, and chemical substance. Used for therapeutic class reporting, HEDIS pharmacy measures, population health analytics, and international drug classification alignment.
The date on which the current AWP unit price became effective. Used to apply the correct AWP for historical claims repricing, audit reconciliation, and retroactive rebate calculations.

The Average Wholesale Price (AWP) per unit of the drug as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. AWP is the benchmark pricing reference used in pharmacy reimbursement calculations, MAC pricing comparisons, and rebate contract negotiations. Expressed in USD per unit (tablet, mL, gram, etc.).

The proprietary trade name under which the drug is marketed by the manufacturer (e.g., Lipitor, Humira). Used in formulary tier assignment, rebate contracting, member communications, and brand vs. generic substitution logic in PBM adjudication.

The DEA controlled substance schedule classification (I through V) indicating the drug's potential for abuse and accepted medical use. Schedule II-V drugs require enhanced DUR edits, quantity limits, early refill restrictions, and special handling in PBM adjudication. Not_scheduled indicates no DEA restriction.

The timestamp when this drug master record was first created in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and change data capture tracking per HIPAA Security Rule requirements.

Indicates whether a valid DEA registration number is required on the prescription for this drug due to its controlled substance classification. Drives prescription validation edits in PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing workflows.

The physical form in which the drug is dispensed (e.g., tablet, capsule, solution, injection, patch, inhaler). Critical for formulary configuration, quantity limit rules, step therapy protocols, and DUR screening in RxClaim/Argus. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tablet|capsule|solution|injection|patch|inhaler|cream|ointment|suspension|powder — promote to reference product]

The classification code for the drug class within the GPI or ETC hierarchy, representing a more granular grouping below therapeutic subclass (e.g., specific statin subtype). Used in formulary step therapy rules, DUR clinical edits, and rebate tier segmentation in PBM systems.

The human-readable name of the drug class corresponding to the drug_class_code. Provides the most granular named classification level in the therapeutic hierarchy for formulary configuration and clinical reporting.

The full proprietary or established name of the drug as it appears on the FDA-approved label and in the First Databank or Medi-Span drug database feed. Used as the primary human-readable identity label across formulary displays, EOB communications, and member-facing materials.

Current lifecycle status of the drug record in the master file. Active drugs are eligible for formulary placement and PBM adjudication. Discontinued or recalled drugs trigger claim edits and member/provider notifications. Drives formulary maintenance workflows in RxClaim/Argus.

Classification of the drug as brand-name, generic, biosimilar, over-the-counter (OTC), compounded, or repackaged. Drives formulary tier assignment, cost-sharing rules, generic substitution logic, and rebate eligibility in PBM adjudication and formulary management.

The date on which this drug master record became effective and eligible for use in formulary configuration, PBM adjudication, and claims processing. Used for temporal validity enforcement in adjudication date-of-service lookups and formulary effective date management.

The current FDA regulatory approval status of the drug product. Approved drugs are eligible for formulary coverage. Tentatively approved generics may be covered under specific plan designs. Withdrawn or not-approved drugs are excluded from formulary and trigger claim denials. Required for CMS Part D formulary compliance.

The International Nonproprietary Name (INN) or United States Adopted Name (USAN) for the active ingredient(s) of the drug (e.g., atorvastatin, adalimumab). Used for generic substitution logic, DUR screening, therapeutic equivalence grouping, and HEDIS pharmacy measure reporting.

The 14-digit Medi-Span Generic Product Identifier (GPI) code providing a hierarchical drug classification from drug group (2 digits) through drug class, subclass, name, name extension, dosage form, and strength. Used as the primary therapeutic classification key in RxClaim/Argus PBM systems for formulary management, DUR screening, and rebate contracting.

Indicates whether this drug is clinically associated with one or more CMS Hierarchical Condition Categories (HCCs) for risk adjustment purposes. HCC-relevant drugs are used in RAF score modeling, RAPS/EDPS encounter data submissions, and population health stratification for Medicare Advantage plans.

Indicates whether this drug is an FDA-approved biosimilar to a reference biological product. Biosimilars have distinct formulary placement rules, interchangeability designations, and rebate contracting considerations under CMS Part D and commercial plan designs.

Indicates whether an FDA-approved generic equivalent is available in the market for this brand-name drug. Used in formulary tier assignment, member cost-sharing communications, and generic substitution prompts during PBM adjudication and pharmacy dispensing.

Indicates whether this drug is classified as a maintenance medication intended for long-term chronic condition management (e.g., antihypertensives, statins, diabetes medications). Maintenance drugs are eligible for 90-day supply dispensing at mail-order pharmacies and are used in HEDIS medication adherence measures (PDC).

Indicates whether this drug qualifies as a preventive medication under ACA Section 2713 or HDHP/HSA preventive care rules, making it eligible for $0 cost-sharing before the deductible is met. Used in benefit configuration and claims adjudication cost-sharing logic.

Indicates whether this drug is classified as a specialty pharmaceutical requiring special handling, storage, administration, or monitoring (e.g., biologics, oncology agents, hepatitis C treatments). Specialty drugs are subject to mandatory specialty pharmacy channel requirements, prior authorization, and enhanced DUR in PBM adjudication. Critical for CMS Part D specialty tier placement.

The 5-digit FDA-assigned labeler code (first segment of the NDC) identifying the firm that manufactures, repackages, relabels, or distributes the drug. Used for manufacturer-level rebate aggregation and PBM contract management.

The FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=originator brand with no generic, M=multi-source (generic available), N=no current market (discontinued), Y=single-source generic. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary tier assignment, and MAC pricing eligibility determination.

The 11-digit National Drug Code assigned by the FDA uniquely identifying the labeler, product, and package size of a drug. This is the primary external business identifier for the drug and is used in pharmacy claims (NCPDP/837P), formulary management, PBM adjudication (RxClaim/Argus), and CMS Part D reporting. Stored in 5-4-2 format without hyphens.
The NDC formatted in the standard 5-4-2 hyphenated display format (e.g., 12345-6789-01) as required for human-readable labeling, SBC documentation, and regulatory submissions to CMS and state DOIs.

The quantity of drug units contained in the dispensed package as defined by the NDC (e.g., 30 tablets, 100 mL, 1 vial). Used in unit-of-use pricing calculations, days supply verification, quantity limit enforcement, and MAC pricing per-unit derivation.

The unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., EA for each/tablet/capsule, ML for milliliter, GM for gram). Used in conjunction with package_size for accurate dispensing quantity calculations, days supply verification, and unit-cost pricing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH|KIT — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The pharmacological mechanism-of-action category of the drug (e.g., ACE Inhibitor, Beta Blocker, SSRI, Statin, Monoclonal Antibody). Used for DUR drug-drug interaction screening, therapeutic duplication edits, clinical program targeting in care management, and HEDIS measure attribution.

The NDC of the FDA Reference Listed Drug (RLD) or reference biological product to which this generic or biosimilar is therapeutically equivalent. Enables generic substitution relationship mapping, step therapy configuration, and biosimilar interchangeability tracking in the PBM system.

business_glossary_term: Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) Program
description: The name of the FDA-mandated Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS) program associated with this drug, if applicable (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin, TIRF REMS for fentanyl products). REMS drugs require special dispensing controls, patient enrollment, and prescriber certification that must be validated during PBM adjudication and prior authorization.

The path by which the drug is administered to the patient (e.g., oral, intravenous, subcutaneous, topical, inhalation). Drives formulary benefit category assignment (medical vs. pharmacy benefit), prior authorization requirements, and specialty pharmacy routing in PBM adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: oral|intravenous|subcutaneous|intramuscular|topical|inhalation|transdermal|ophthalmic|otic|rectal — promote to reference product]

The native record identifier from the originating source system (First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim, or Argus) for this drug master record. Used for data lineage, cross-system reconciliation, and upstream change tracking in the lakehouse ingestion pipeline.

Identifies the operational source system from which this drug master record was ingested (e.g., FDB=First Databank, MEDISPAN=Medi-Span, RXCLAIM=RxClaim PBM, ARGUS=Argus PBM, INTERNAL=internally maintained). Used for data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during ETL, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver layer.

The concentration or potency of the active ingredient(s) expressed as a numeric value with unit of measure (e.g., 10 mg, 500 mg/5 mL, 0.25%). Used in DUR duplicate therapy screening, quantity limit enforcement, and clinical appropriateness review in UM/PA workflows.

The date on which this drug master record was terminated or superseded, indicating the drug is no longer active for formulary placement or new claim adjudication. Null indicates the record is currently active. Used for historical claims repricing and formulary audit trails.

The standardized code identifying the broad therapeutic class of the drug (e.g., cardiovascular agents, anti-infectives, antineoplastics). Used for formulary tier grouping, rebate contract aggregation, utilization reporting, and disease management program enrollment triggers.

The human-readable name of the therapeutic class corresponding to the therapeutic_class_code. Used in formulary displays, member-facing SBC documents, provider communications, and regulatory reporting to CMS and state DOIs.

The FDA Orange Book therapeutic equivalence evaluation code (e.g., AB, AA, BC) indicating whether a generic drug is therapeutically equivalent to the reference listed drug. AB-rated generics are substitutable at the pharmacy. Used in generic substitution logic, formulary step therapy, and DAW (Dispense As Written) override processing in PBM adjudication.

The standardized code identifying the therapeutic subclass within the broader therapeutic class (e.g., HMG-CoA Reductase Inhibitors within cardiovascular agents). Enables granular formulary tier differentiation, step therapy protocol design, and rebate contract segmentation.

The human-readable name of the therapeutic subclass corresponding to the therapeutic_subclass_code. Used in formulary management workflows, clinical program configuration, and detailed utilization analytics.

The timestamp when this drug master record was last modified in the lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental data refresh, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse pipeline.

The Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit as reported by the manufacturer, representing the list price to wholesalers before discounts and rebates. Used as a pricing benchmark for specialty drug reimbursement, rebate baseline calculations, and CMS Part D coverage gap discount program calculations.
Unique surrogate identifier for each NDC-level drug pricing record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the drug_pricing data product.

Foreign key linking to pharmacy.drug_master. Business justification: drug_pricing records are defined per NDC; linking to drug_master provides the authoritative drug attributes and eliminates duplicated columns.

Percentage discount applied to AWP to derive the reimbursement rate under AWP-based contracts (e.g., AWP minus 15%). Expressed as a positive decimal percentage (e.g., 15.0000 = 15%). Used for PBM contract compliance validation and reimbursement audits.
Average Wholesale Price per unit as published by First Databank or Medi-Span. Commonly used as the benchmark for pharmacy reimbursement calculations and discount negotiations. Expressed in USD.

Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Drug pricing tables are derived from PBM contract agreements; linking ensures price source traceability for regulatory filings and rebate calculations.
Timestamp when this drug pricing record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring of pricing file processing.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary price fields in this record (e.g., USD). Supports multi-currency operations for Puerto Rico and other US territories. Defaults to USD for domestic plans.
Number of days of drug supply for which this pricing record applies (e.g., 30-day retail, 90-day mail order). Used to differentiate pricing tiers by supply duration per PBM contract terms.

DEA controlled substance schedule classification (CI through CV) or NON-CONTROLLED. Used for DUR edits, quantity limit enforcement, prior authorization requirements, and regulatory compliance reporting.

Pharmacy dispensing channel for which this pricing record applies: retail (community pharmacy), mail_order (90-day supply mail service), specialty (specialty pharmacy), or long_term_care (LTC facility pharmacy). Pricing may differ by channel per PBM contract terms.

Fixed dispensing fee in USD paid to the pharmacy per prescription fill, separate from the ingredient cost. Defined in PBM network contracts and applied during claim adjudication to calculate total pharmacy reimbursement.

Date on which this pricing record becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication. Used to select the correct price for a claim based on the date of service. Aligns with PBM pricing file effective dates.

Date on which this pricing record expires and is no longer applicable for new claim adjudication. Null indicates an open-ended pricing record with no defined end date. Used with effective_date to determine the active pricing window.

Formulary tier assignment for this drug under the applicable plan benefit design (e.g., tier_1=preferred generic, tier_2=generic, tier_3=preferred brand, tier_4=non-preferred brand, tier_5=specialty). Drives member cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) during claim adjudication.

Version identifier of the MAC list, used to track updates and revisions to the MAC pricing schedule. Supports audit trails for MAC list changes and pharmacy reimbursement disputes. Null when price_type is not MAC.

Describes the methodology used to derive the MAC price (e.g., MARKET_BASKET=based on market survey of acquisition costs, NADAC_BASED=derived from CMS NADAC data, AWP_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from AWP, WAC_DISCOUNT=percentage discount from WAC, CUSTOM=plan-specific methodology). Required for PBM contract compliance and regulatory transparency. Null when price_type is not MAC.

Maximum Allowable Cost per unit set by the PBM or health plan for generic and multi-source drugs. Represents the upper reimbursement limit for a drug under the applicable MAC list. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not MAC.

FDA Orange Book multi-source code indicating the competitive market status of the drug: O=Originator brand (no generic), M=Multi-source (generic available), N=Non-multi-source, Y=Generic product. Used to determine MAC eligibility and generic substitution applicability.

Quantity of units contained in the standard package as defined by the NDC. Used to convert between package-level and per-unit pricing and to validate dispensed quantity against package size during adjudication.

Unit of measure for the package size quantity (e.g., TAB for tablets, ML for milliliters). Distinct from unit_of_measure which applies to the pricing unit; this field describes the package-level quantity unit. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]

Percentage change in unit price from the prior pricing record to the current record, calculated as ((unit_price - prior_unit_price) / prior_unit_price) * 100. Used for drug price inflation monitoring, CMS price increase reporting, and PBM contract compliance.

Classifies the pricing methodology represented by this record: AWP (Average Wholesale Price), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost), MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), RBP (Reference Based Pricing), NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary. Determines how the price is applied during claim adjudication and reimbursement calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AWP|WAC|MAC|RBP|NADAC|USUAL_AND_CUSTOMARY — promote to reference product]

Publication or release date of the source pricing file from which this record was extracted. Used to track pricing file currency, identify stale pricing data, and support audit and reconciliation processes.

Name of the source pricing file from which this record was loaded (e.g., 'FDB_PRICE_20240101.txt', 'RXCLAIM_MAC_Q1_2024.csv'). Used for data lineage, audit trails, and troubleshooting pricing discrepancies.

Identifies the originating source of the pricing data (e.g., First Databank, Medi-Span, RxClaim PBM feed, Argus PBM feed, CMS published rates, internal custom pricing). Critical for audit trails and PBM contract compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FDB|MEDISPAN|RXCLAIM|ARGUS|CMS|INTERNAL|CUSTOM — promote to reference product]

Current lifecycle status of the pricing record: active (currently in use for adjudication), inactive (no longer applicable), pending (approved but not yet effective), superseded (replaced by a newer pricing record for the same NDC and price type).
Previous per-unit price for this NDC and price type before the most recent pricing update. Used for price change analysis, trend reporting, and PBM contract compliance audits.
Reference Based Pricing per unit used as the benchmark reimbursement amount under RBP pharmacy contracts. Expressed in USD. Null when price_type is not RBP.

Dispensing unit of measure for which the price applies (e.g., EA=Each, ML=Milliliter, GM=Gram, MG=Milligram, UN=Unit, TAB=Tablet, CAP=Capsule, VIAL=Vial, PATCH=Patch). Required for accurate per-unit cost calculation during adjudication. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|ML|GM|MG|UN|TAB|CAP|VIAL|PATCH — promote to reference product]

Per-unit drug price expressed in USD for the applicable price type (AWP, WAC, MAC, or RBP). Used as the basis for claim adjudication, cost-sharing calculation, and pharmacy reimbursement. Precision to 6 decimal places to accommodate high-precision specialty drug pricing.
Timestamp when this drug pricing record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.

Wholesale Acquisition Cost per unit as reported by the manufacturer to the pricing compendia. Represents the list price from manufacturer to wholesaler before discounts. Expressed in USD. Used in WAC-based reimbursement contracts.
Brand name under which the drug is marketed.
Physical form of the drug (e.g., tablet, capsule).
Unit of measurement for dosage strength.
Category of the drug (e.g., pain management, cardiovascular).
Class of the drug (e.g., analgesic, antibiotic).
Standard code identifying the drug (e.g., RxNorm code).
Type of drug code used (RxNorm, NDC, or other).
Code representing the dosage form of the drug.
Current lifecycle status of the drug.
Code representing the therapeutic class of the drug.
The effective date for this record.
Timestamp when the MAC record was created in the system.
Identifier of the user who created the MAC record.
Currency in which the MAC value is expressed.
Date when the MAC value becomes effective.
Date when the MAC value expires.
The mac list name for this record.
The mac methodology for this record.
The mac price for this record.
Source of the MAC value (PBM, manufacturer, CMS).
Date when the MAC value was sourced.
The mac status for this record.
The mac termination date for this record.
The mac unit price for this record.
Timestamp when the MAC record was last updated.
Identifier of the user who last updated the MAC record.
Maximum allowable cost for the drug as per PBM contract.
The multi source indicator for this record.
Standard 10-digit NDC code identifying the drug.
Unit of measurement for package size.
Numeric value of the package size.
The pricing basis for this record.
Timestamp when the record was first captured in the audit trail.
Identifier of the user who created the record in the audit trail.
Timestamp when the record was last updated in the audit trail.
Identifier of the user who updated the record in the audit trail.
The source system code for this record.
The termination date for this record.
The unit of measure for this record.
The updated timestamp for this record.
The mac list version for this record.
Identifier for the employer group or sponsor associated with the member's pharmacy benefit. Relevant for group-level accumulator reporting and ASO/TPA arrangements.
Identifier for the health benefit plan under which the member is enrolled, determining the applicable deductible, OOP maximum, and Part D benefit phase thresholds.
Unique identifier for the health plan member whose pharmacy benefit accumulators are being tracked. Links to the member master record in the enrollment system (Facets/QNXT).
Identifier of the most recent pharmacy claim that triggered an update to this accumulator record. Supports audit trail and real-time adjudication reconciliation.
Date on which the accumulator balances were last reset to zero, typically at the start of a new benefit year. Supports annual reconciliation and audit of accumulator lifecycle.

Identifies the operational system of record that originated or last updated this accumulator record (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM, Facets, QNXT). Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail requirements.

Current lifecycle state of the accumulator record. 'active' indicates in-use for real-time adjudication; 'suspended' indicates member eligibility gap; 'closed' indicates benefit period ended; 'pending_reset' indicates awaiting annual reset; 'reconciled' indicates CMS or plan reconciliation completed.

Optimistic locking version counter incremented with each update to the accumulator record. Supports concurrent adjudication event handling and prevents race conditions in real-time point-of-sale updates.
Last date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Defines the boundary for annual accumulator reset.
First date of the benefit year or coverage period to which this accumulator record applies. Accumulators reset at the start of each new benefit period.
Cumulative drug cost amounts applied toward the Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold. Tracks member spend after exiting the coverage gap and entering catastrophic coverage.

The Medicare Part D catastrophic coverage threshold amount for the current benefit year. Once TrOOP reaches this level, the member qualifies for catastrophic cost-sharing rates. Published annually by CMS.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Reconciliation Status
description: Status of the CMS annual Part D reconciliation process for this accumulator record. Tracks whether the accumulator data has been submitted to and accepted by CMS via RAPS/EDPS for risk adjustment and subsidy reconciliation.
Indicates the coordination of benefits (COB) role of this plan for the member's pharmacy benefit. Determines how accumulator amounts are applied when multiple payers are involved.

Cumulative manufacturer discount amounts applied to brand-name drugs while the member is in the Medicare Part D coverage gap (donut hole). These discounts count toward TrOOP under the ACA Coverage Gap Discount Program.
Date and time when this accumulator record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Cumulative dollar amount the member has applied toward their pharmacy deductible in the current benefit period. Updated with each pharmacy claim adjudication event. Used at point of sale to determine member cost-sharing responsibility.
Total pharmacy deductible amount the member must satisfy before plan cost-sharing begins, as defined in the benefit plan design for the current benefit period.

Indicates whether an Explanation of Benefits (EOB) has been generated for the member reflecting the current accumulator balances. Supports member communication and regulatory EOB delivery requirements.

Cumulative dollar amount applied toward the family-level pharmacy deductible for plans with embedded or aggregate family deductible structures. Applicable for commercial group plans with family coverage tiers.
Cumulative out-of-pocket spend applied toward the family-level MOOP for plans with family OOP limits. Tracks combined member cost-sharing across all family members enrolled under the same plan.

The formulary drug tier level most recently applied during adjudication for this member's accumulator. Indicates the cost-sharing tier structure in effect, which affects deductible and OOP accumulation rates.

business_glossary_term: Health Savings Account (HSA) Eligible Applied Amount
description: Cumulative amount of pharmacy cost-sharing that qualifies as HSA-eligible expenses under IRS rules for members enrolled in a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). Supports member HSA contribution and tax reporting.

Cumulative total drug costs (plan paid plus member cost-sharing) applied toward the Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL). When this amount reaches the ICL threshold, the member enters the coverage gap (donut hole).

The Medicare Part D Initial Coverage Limit (ICL) threshold amount for the current benefit year, as published annually by CMS. Defines the boundary between the initial coverage phase and the coverage gap (donut hole).

Indicates whether the member has fully satisfied their pharmacy deductible for the current benefit period. When true, plan cost-sharing (copay/coinsurance) applies rather than full deductible responsibility.

Indicates whether the member has reached their maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit for the current benefit period. When true, the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period.
Date and time of the most recent pharmacy claim adjudication event that updated this accumulator record. Critical for real-time point-of-sale cost-sharing calculation and concurrency control.

The line of business (LOB) under which the member's pharmacy benefit is administered. Determines applicable regulatory framework, benefit phase rules, and accumulator logic (e.g., Part D coverage gap applies only to Medicare Part D).
The applicable LIS copay tier for the member, determining the fixed copay amounts for generic, preferred brand, non-preferred, and specialty drugs under the Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) program.

Low-Income Subsidy (LIS) eligibility level for Medicare Part D members, determining reduced cost-sharing amounts. 'full' indicates 100% subsidy; 'partial_1/2/3' indicate graduated subsidy tiers; 'none' indicates no LIS. Affects TrOOP accumulation rules.
Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied for drugs dispensed through mail-order pharmacy channels in the current benefit period. Supports mail-order utilization reporting and member EOB generation.

The maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) dollar threshold the member must reach before the plan covers 100% of covered pharmacy costs for the remainder of the benefit period. Set per plan design and ACA/CMS requirements.

Cumulative out-of-pocket (OOP) spend by the member for pharmacy benefits in the current benefit period, including deductible, copays, and coinsurance. Used to track progress toward the maximum out-of-pocket (MOOP) limit.
Current Medicare Part D benefit phase for the member based on accumulated TrOOP and ICL amounts. Drives cost-sharing calculation at point of sale. 'not_applicable' for non-Part D lines of business.
Member identifier assigned by the Pharmacy Benefit Manager (PBM) system (RxClaim or Argus). Used to cross-reference accumulator balances between the health plan and PBM for reconciliation.
Total number of pharmacy claims adjudicated and applied to this accumulator record in the current benefit period. Used for utilization reporting and EOB generation.

Cumulative cost-sharing amount applied specifically for specialty tier drugs in the current benefit period. Tracked separately to support specialty pharmacy reporting, prior authorization analytics, and plan design evaluation.

Cumulative dollar amount paid by third parties (e.g., manufacturer copay assistance programs, other insurance) toward the member's pharmacy cost-sharing. Tracked separately as these amounts may not count toward TrOOP under CMS rules.

Cumulative true out-of-pocket (TrOOP) expenditure for Medicare Part D members, counting only qualifying cost-sharing amounts toward the catastrophic coverage threshold. Excludes amounts paid by third parties not qualifying under CMS TrOOP rules. Required for CMS Part D benefit phase determination.
The annual TrOOP threshold amount that, when reached, transitions a Medicare Part D member into catastrophic coverage. Published annually by CMS as part of the Part D benefit parameters.
Date and time when this accumulator record was most recently modified, reflecting the latest claim adjudication event or manual adjustment. Supports concurrency control and audit trail.
Unique surrogate identifier for the formulary record. Primary key for the formulary data product in the pharmacy domain.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_obligation. Business justification: Formulary design must adhere to specific regulatory obligations (ACA, CMS); linking enables reporting of compliance status per formulary.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Formulary management assigns a responsible pharmacist/employee; needed for formulary change approval and governance reporting.
Reference to the immediately preceding version of this formulary. Enables formulary change tracking, member notification workflows, and historical adjudication lookups for appeals and grievances.
Date on which member notification was sent for formulary changes. CMS Part D requires 60-day advance notice for non-preferred formulary changes and immediate notification for safety-related removals.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Formulary Identifier
description: The CMS-assigned formulary identifier used in Part D formulary file submissions to CMS. Required for Medicare Part D plans to identify the formulary in RAPS and EDPS submissions.
Timestamp when this formulary record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance documentation purposes.

Total number of unique drug entries (NDC or drug-tier combinations) included in this formulary at the time of the last update. Used for formulary adequacy assessments and CMS Part D formulary file validation.

Indicates whether Drug Utilization Review (DUR) edits are applied during adjudication under this formulary. DUR programs screen for drug interactions, duplicate therapy, and inappropriate dosing per OBRA '90 requirements.
The date on which this formulary becomes active and applicable to member benefit adjudication. Used by PBM systems (RxClaim/Argus) to determine which formulary version applies at point of dispensing.

Business classification of the formulary indicating its benefit richness level relative to other formularies offered by the plan. Used in benefit design analytics, member communications, and plan comparison tools.

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the formulary across systems, used in EDI transactions, PBM interfaces, and CMS Part D submissions. Corresponds to the formulary ID field in RxClaim/Argus PBM system.

Free-text business description of the formulary, including its intended population, key design features, and any notable coverage inclusions or exclusions. Used in internal documentation and plan benefit configuration.

Human-readable business name of the formulary (e.g., 'Standard Commercial Formulary 2025', 'Medicare Part D Enhanced Formulary'). Used in member-facing materials, SBC documents, and plan benefit configurations.

Current lifecycle status of the formulary record. Active formularies are used in live adjudication; pending formularies are approved but not yet effective; retired formularies are historical records no longer in use.

Classification of the formulary structure indicating coverage breadth and tier design. Open formularies cover most drugs; closed formularies restrict to listed drugs only; tiered formularies apply differential cost-sharing by tier.

Policy governing generic drug substitution at point of dispensing. Mandatory substitution requires dispensing of generic equivalents; permissive allows brand if requested; DAW (Dispense As Written) honored respects prescriber instructions.

Indicates whether this formulary meets ACA Qualified Health Plan (QHP) essential health benefit (EHB) drug coverage requirements, including the required drug categories and classes under 45 CFR Part 156.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Part D Indicator
description: Indicates whether this formulary is subject to CMS Medicare Part D regulatory requirements, including annual bid submission, formulary file submission to CMS, and coverage determination obligations.
Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee last reviewed and approved this formulary. P&T Committee review is required for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation.

Line of Business (LOB) associated with this formulary, indicating the regulatory and market segment under which the formulary operates. Drives CMS Part D, Medicaid, or commercial compliance requirements.

Indicates whether this formulary is designated for use by Low Income Subsidy (LIS) eligible members under Medicare Part D. LIS formularies must meet CMS benchmark plan requirements for cost-sharing and drug coverage.

Indicates whether this formulary supports mail-order pharmacy dispensing. Mail-order programs typically offer 90-day supplies at reduced cost-sharing and are a key component of pharmacy benefit design.
Scheduled date for the next Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee review of this formulary. Supports compliance tracking for CMS Part D requirements mandating regular formulary review cycles.

The formulary identifier as assigned by the PBM system (RxClaim or Argus). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the health plan's internal formulary record and the PBM's formulary configuration.
The benefit plan year (e.g., 2025) for which this formulary is effective. Used for annual formulary change management, CMS Part D annual bid submissions, and benefit year reporting.
Indicates whether this formulary requires prior authorization (PA) for any covered drugs. PA requirements are subject to CMS Part D coverage determination timelines and member appeal rights.

business_glossary_term: Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee Approval Date
description: Date on which the Pharmacy and Therapeutics (P&T) Committee formally approved this formulary or the most recent formulary update. Required documentation for CMS Part D compliance and NCQA accreditation audits.

Indicates whether this formulary applies quantity limits (QL) on covered drugs, restricting the amount dispensed per fill or per defined time period. Quantity limits are a utilization management tool subject to CMS Part D exception requirements.
The date on which CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance approved this formulary for use. Required for Part D compliance documentation and audit trails.
The date on which the formulary was submitted to CMS or the applicable State Department of Insurance for regulatory review and approval.

Status of the formulary's regulatory filing with CMS or applicable State Department of Insurance (DOI). Tracks whether the formulary has been submitted, approved, or rejected for compliance with Part D or state market requirements.

Identifies the operational source system from which this formulary record originated (e.g., RxClaim, Argus PBM system, Facets). Used for data lineage tracking and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.

Indicates whether this formulary designates a specialty pharmacy network for dispensing high-cost specialty drugs. Specialty pharmacy programs may include limited distribution networks and enhanced clinical support services.

Indicates whether this formulary includes a specialty drug tier for high-cost specialty medications. Specialty tiers typically apply to drugs with monthly costs exceeding CMS specialty tier threshold (currently $670/month for Part D).

Indicates whether this formulary employs step therapy (also known as fail-first) protocols requiring members to try lower-cost or preferred drugs before coverage of higher-cost alternatives is authorized.
The date on which this formulary is no longer active. Null indicates an open-ended formulary with no defined end date. Used for formulary version management and historical adjudication lookups.

The total number of cost-sharing tiers defined in this formulary (e.g., 3-tier, 4-tier, 5-tier, 6-tier). Determines the tier structure used in drug tier assignments and member cost-sharing calculations.
Timestamp when this formulary record was last modified. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads in the Databricks Silver layer, and audit trail requirements.

Public-facing URL where members can access the formulary drug list. CMS Part D and ACA QHP regulations require health plans to publish formularies online and provide the URL in plan materials and SBC documents.

Version number of the formulary, incremented with each approved update or mid-year formulary change. Used to track formulary change history and ensure members receive required 60-day advance notice of non-preferred changes.

Unique surrogate identifier for each formulary drug tier assignment record. Primary key for this association entity linking a drug (NDC) to a formulary with its tier, cost-sharing, and utilization management (UM) controls.
Reference to the internal drug master record corresponding to this NDC, enabling joins to the drug master for drug name, therapeutic class, and other drug attributes.
Reference to the formulary to which this drug tier assignment belongs. A formulary is the approved list of covered drugs for a given plan and benefit year.
Reference to the health plan or benefit plan under which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. Links the tier assignment to the specific plan for cost-sharing and benefit configuration.
The plan benefit year (e.g., 2024, 2025) to which this formulary drug tier assignment applies. CMS Part D formulary files are submitted annually by benefit year.

Member coinsurance percentage applied to the drug cost under this formulary tier assignment, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.20 = 20%). Used when cost-sharing is percentage-based rather than fixed copay. Applies to specialty and high-cost tiers.

Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed through a mail-order pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Mail-order copays are typically lower than retail to incentivize mail-order utilization.
Fixed member copay amount for a 30-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. Used in SBC and EOB generation.
Fixed member copay amount for a 90-day supply dispensed at a retail pharmacy under this formulary tier assignment. Applicable for maintenance medications at extended-supply retail pharmacies.

Indicates whether the drug is covered under this formulary assignment. 'covered' = fully covered per tier rules; 'covered_with_restrictions' = covered subject to UM controls (PA, ST, QL); 'not_covered' = not on formulary; 'excluded' = explicitly excluded per CMS or plan rules.
Timestamp when this formulary drug tier assignment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance documentation.
Indicates whether the plan deductible must be met before cost-sharing for this drug tier applies. Relevant for High Deductible Health Plans (HDHP) and plans with drug-specific deductibles.

Pharmacy dispensing channel(s) through which this drug may be dispensed under this formulary tier assignment. 'specialty' indicates dispensing is restricted to a specialty pharmacy network. Drives pharmacy routing at point of sale.

Business reason for a change to the drug's tier assignment (e.g., 'generic available', 'contract renegotiation', 'clinical review', 'CMS requirement', 'formulary refresh'). Supports audit trail and CMS formulary change notification requirements.

Type of Drug Utilization Review (DUR) alert associated with this formulary drug tier assignment (e.g., 'drug-drug interaction', 'drug-disease contraindication', 'duplicate therapy', 'dose range check'). Drives DUR edits at point of sale in the PBM adjudication system. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drug_drug_interaction|drug_disease|duplicate_therapy|dose_range|drug_allergy|drug_age — promote to reference product]

Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment becomes effective. Used to determine the applicable tier and cost-sharing rules at the time of dispensing. Required for CMS formulary file submissions.

Indicates whether a quantity limit (QL) applies to this drug under this formulary assignment. When true, the dispensed quantity per fill or per time period is restricted. Reported in the CMS QL extract file.

Date on which this formulary drug tier assignment expires or is terminated. Null indicates the assignment is currently active with no defined end date. Supports historical tracking and CMS formulary file change submissions.

Human-readable label for the drug tier (e.g., 'Generic', 'Preferred Brand', 'Non-Preferred Brand', 'Specialty', 'Specialty Preferred'). Displayed on member-facing materials including the Summary of Benefits and Coverage (SBC) and Explanation of Benefits (EOB).

Numeric tier level assigned to the drug within the formulary (e.g., 1=Generic, 2=Preferred Brand, 3=Non-Preferred Brand, 4=Specialty, 5=Specialty Preferred, 6=Select Care). Drives member cost-sharing at point of sale. CMS requires tier number in formulary file submissions.
