Legal name of the pharmaceutical manufacturer obligated to pay the rebate. Retained on the transaction for reporting and reconciliation without requiring a join.
Drug's market share within its therapeutic class during the rebate period, expressed as a decimal percentage. Used to determine market share rebate tier attainment per contract.

Classification of the rebate for ACA Medical Loss Ratio (MLR) reporting purposes: medical_cost_reduction (reduces incurred claims), quality_improvement (allocated to QI activities), or excluded (not counted in MLR numerator).
11-digit National Drug Code identifying the specific drug product (labeler, product, package) subject to the rebate. Formatted as 5-4-2 per FDA NDC Directory standards.
Indicates whether this rebate is subject to CMS Part D rebate pass-through requirements. True for Medicare Part D covered drugs; False for commercial or Medicaid lines of business.
Portion of the received rebate amount that must be passed through to CMS or plan members per Part D or ACA requirements. Critical for MLR and CMS DIR (Direct and Indirect Remuneration) reporting.
Contractual date by which the manufacturer or PBM must remit the rebate payment. Used for aging analysis and dispute escalation.
Date on which the rebate payment was actually received and posted. Null if payment has not yet been received.
Indicates whether the contractual performance target (e.g., market share threshold, utilization goal) was met during the rebate period, triggering performance rebate eligibility.
Indicates whether prior authorization was required for the drug during the rebate period. PA requirements can affect utilization volumes and rebate performance thresholds.
Externally-known invoice number assigned by the PBM or manufacturer for this rebate billing cycle. Used for reconciliation with accounts receivable and PBM remittance.
Last day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period for which this rebate invoice applies.
First day of the rebate performance or utilization measurement period (typically a calendar quarter) for which this rebate invoice applies.
Price benchmark on which the contracted rebate rate is applied: Average Wholesale Price (AWP), Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC), Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC), or net price.

Classification of the rebate by its contractual basis: base (standard formulary placement), performance (quality/utilization targets), market_share (volume threshold), formulary_access (preferred tier), inflation (price increase above CPI), or supplemental (ad hoc negotiated). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base|performance|market_share|formulary_access|inflation|supplemental — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]
Actual cash amount received from the manufacturer or PBM for this rebate invoice. Used for cash reconciliation and MLR reporting.

Current lifecycle status of the rebate invoice reconciliation process: pending (not yet invoiced), invoiced (invoice sent), partially_received (partial payment), received (fully paid), disputed (discrepancy under review), written_off (uncollectable). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|invoiced|partially_received|received|disputed|written_off — promote to reference product]

Identifies the operational source system from which this rebate record originated: RxClaim, Argus (PBM platforms), or manual (spreadsheet/manual entry). Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.
Indicates whether the drug is classified as a specialty drug, which typically carries higher rebate rates and requires prior authorization. Specialty drugs are subject to distinct PBM contract terms.
Indicates whether step therapy protocols were in effect for this drug during the rebate period. Step therapy requirements influence market share and performance rebate attainment.
Pharmacological therapeutic class code (e.g., GPI or ATC code) for the drug, used to aggregate rebates by drug category for formulary strategy and analytics.
Timestamp when this drug rebate record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes during incremental Silver layer processing.
Number of pharmacy claims adjudicated for the drug during the rebate period. Used to validate utilization units and support audit of rebate calculations.
Total dispensed units (e.g., tablets, milliliters, grams) of the drug during the rebate period, as reported to the manufacturer for rebate calculation. Sourced from PBM claims data.
Difference between the invoiced amount and the received amount. A non-zero variance triggers reconciliation or dispute resolution workflows.
Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) per unit for the drug as of the rebate period. WAC is the manufacturer's list price and an alternative rebate rate basis to AWP.
Unique surrogate identifier for the pharmacy contract record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the dispensing pharmacy or pharmacy chain entity in the pharmacy master. Links this contract to the specific pharmacy party.

Identifier of the MAC pricing list applied under this contract for generic and multi-source drug reimbursement. References the specific MAC schedule version used by the PBM or health plan. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is mac.
Reference to the PBM contract under which this pharmacy dispensing agreement is administered, when the contract is PBM-administered rather than direct. Null for direct health-plan contracts.

Foreign key linking to network.provider_network. Business justification: Contracts are negotiated per network; linking contracts to provider_network enables contract‑level reporting, tiering, and compliance tracking.

Type of accreditation required or held by the pharmacy under this contract. URAC and ACHC are common for specialty pharmacies. PCAB (Pharmacy Compounding Accreditation Board) for compounding pharmacies. Relevant for specialty and mail-order contracts.
Contractually stipulated frequency at which the health plan or PBM may conduct pharmacy audits. Applicable when audit_rights_indicator is true.
Indicates whether the health plan or PBM retains the right to conduct on-site or desk audits of the pharmacy's dispensing records under this contract. Required for CMS Part D compliance.
Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the required notice period.

Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug's Average Wholesale Price (AWP) to determine ingredient cost reimbursement for brand and generic drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage (e.g., 16.50 = AWP minus 16.50%). Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount.

Externally-known, human-readable contract reference number assigned by the health plan or PBM. Used in correspondence, audits, and regulatory filings. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this agreement.

Current lifecycle state of the pharmacy contract. Active contracts are eligible for claim adjudication. Suspended contracts are temporarily ineligible. Terminated/expired contracts are closed. Drives network eligibility checks in RxClaim/Argus.

Classification of the contractual relationship: direct (health plan contracts directly with pharmacy), pbm_administered (PBM manages on plan's behalf), chain (multi-location chain agreement), independent (single-location independent pharmacy), mail_order, or specialty. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct|pbm_administered|chain|independent|mail_order|specialty — promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the pharmacy must complete and maintain credentialing requirements (state licensure, DEA registration, accreditation) as a condition of this contract.
Maximum days supply per mail-order prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 90 days). Drives adjudication edits in RxClaim/Argus.
Maximum days supply per retail prescription fill authorized under this contract (typically 30 or 34 days). Claims exceeding this limit may be rejected during adjudication.

The dispensing channel(s) authorized under this contract. Retail covers walk-in pharmacies; mail_order covers 90-day supply dispensing; specialty covers high-cost/complex drugs; long_term_care covers SNF/LTC facilities; home_infusion covers IV therapy.

Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per mail-order prescription dispensed. Typically lower than retail due to higher volume and automation. Applicable for mail-order and specialty pharmacy contracts.
Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid to the pharmacy per retail prescription dispensed, in addition to ingredient cost reimbursement. A core financial term of the pharmacy contract.
Contracted flat dispensing fee (in USD) paid per specialty drug prescription dispensed. Specialty drugs require enhanced clinical services, justifying a separate fee schedule.
Date on which the pharmacy contract becomes binding and the pharmacy is eligible to dispense claims under this agreement. Used for network eligibility validation during adjudication.
Date on which the pharmacy contract was fully executed (signed by all parties). May precede the effective_date if there is a future start date.

business_glossary_term: Generic Average Wholesale Price (AWP) Discount Percentage
description: Contracted AWP discount percentage specifically for generic drugs, which typically carries a deeper discount than brand drugs. Expressed as a positive percentage. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is awp_discount and a separate generic rate is negotiated.

Contracted minimum generic dispensing rate (GDR) the pharmacy must achieve as a performance requirement. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Supports CMS Part D Star Ratings and cost management objectives.

The pricing methodology used to calculate ingredient cost reimbursement to the pharmacy. AWP (Average Wholesale Price) discount, MAC (Maximum Allowable Cost), WAC (Wholesale Acquisition Cost) discount, NADAC (National Average Drug Acquisition Cost), or Usual and Customary (U&C). Core reimbursement term in the contract.
Date of the most recent amendment or addendum to this pharmacy contract. Used to track contract evolution and ensure the current fee schedule and terms are applied during adjudication.

The line of business to which this pharmacy contract applies. Determines applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., CMS Part D rules for Medicare, state Medicaid rules). A contract may be scoped to one or multiple LOBs.
Indicates whether this pharmacy is authorized to dispense mail-order (90-day supply) prescriptions under this contract.

business_glossary_term: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs (NCPDP) Provider ID
description: 7-digit NCPDP pharmacy identifier used in pharmacy claim transactions (NCPDP Telecom D.0) and PBM adjudication. Complements the NPI for pharmacy-specific identification.

Network participation tier assigned to the pharmacy under this contract. Preferred pharmacies typically offer lower member cost-sharing. Drives member cost-sharing calculations and formulary benefit design.
Indicates whether this pharmacy contract is applicable to Medicare Part D prescription drug benefit administration. When true, CMS Part D network adequacy, pricing, and reporting requirements apply.
Indicates whether this contract includes performance guarantees (e.g., generic dispensing rate, medication adherence, fill accuracy). When true, performance_guarantee_details apply.
10-digit National Provider Identifier assigned to the dispensing pharmacy by CMS. Used for claims adjudication, EDI transactions (NCPDP 5.1/D.0), and network directory publication.
Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and SLA monitoring.
Timestamp when this pharmacy contract record was most recently updated in the lakehouse silver layer. Used for change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.
Indicates whether the contract includes provisions allowing the health plan or PBM to recoup overpayments identified through audits or claims reviews.

Native contract identifier from the originating PBM system (RxClaim or Argus) or provider management system (Cactus/ProviderSource). Used for lineage tracing and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.
Indicates whether this pharmacy is contracted and credentialed to dispense specialty drugs under this agreement. Specialty pharmacies must meet enhanced accreditation requirements (URAC, ACHC).

Two-letter US state code identifying the primary state jurisdiction governing this pharmacy contract. Determines applicable state DOI regulations, MAC transparency laws, and pharmacy contracting statutes.
Date on which the pharmacy contract ends or was terminated. Null for open-ended contracts. Claims with service dates after this date are not eligible for reimbursement under this contract.
Number of calendar days advance written notice required by either party to terminate the contract without cause. Regulatory minimums vary by state DOI requirements.
Version identifier of the pharmacy contract document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, Amendment 3). Tracks contract amendments and renegotiations over time for audit and compliance purposes.
Contracted discount percentage applied to the drug's Wholesale Acquisition Cost (WAC) for ingredient cost reimbursement. Applicable when ingredient_cost_basis is wac_discount.
Unique identifier for the exclusion screening event record.
The system user identifier or employee ID of the individual or automated process that initiated the screening event.
Identifier of the provider being screened against exclusion lists.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this screening event has been flagged for audit review due to anomalies, policy violations, or regulatory examination requirements.
Free-text notes documenting audit findings, reviewer comments, or regulatory examination references related to this screening event.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Reporting Flag
description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this screening event must be included in CMS regulatory reporting submissions or program integrity audits.

The compliance status of this screening event relative to regulatory requirements: compliant (on schedule), overdue (past due date), pending review (awaiting resolution), non-compliant (violation), or waived (exception granted).
The version or publication date of the exclusion list data source used for this screening, ensuring traceability to the specific list snapshot consulted.
The date on which the exclusion became effective, as reported by the exclusion list source. Null if no match found.
The date on which the exclusion is scheduled to end or was lifted, if applicable. Null for indefinite exclusions or no match.
The reason or statutory basis for the exclusion as reported by the exclusion list source (e.g., fraud, patient abuse, license revocation). Null if no match found.
The scope of the exclusion found: Federal (Medicare/Medicaid), State (state-specific Medicaid), Both (federal and state), or None (no exclusion).
Detailed narrative or structured information about the match, including matched data elements, exclusion list entry identifiers, and any discrepancies requiring review.

value_regex: Exact NPI Match|Name and DOB Match|Name and Address Match|Partial Match|No Match
description: The type of match logic that triggered a positive or potential screening result, indicating which data elements matched the exclusion list entry.
The date by which the next exclusion screening is required for this provider to maintain compliance with CMS monthly screening mandates.
The 10-digit National Provider Identifier of the provider being screened, as assigned by CMS.
The date of the most recent prior exclusion screening for this provider, used to track screening cadence and compliance with monthly requirements.
The timestamp when this exclusion screening record was first created in the system.
The timestamp when this exclusion screening record was last modified or updated.

value_regex: No Action Required|Provider Terminated|Contract Suspended|Manual Review Pending|False Positive Confirmed|Waiver Granted
description: The action taken by the health plan in response to the screening result: no action (clear result), provider termination, contract suspension, pending manual review, false positive confirmation, or waiver granted per policy.
The date on which the resolution action was completed or the screening result was finalized. Null if resolution is still pending.
Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, including rationale for the action taken, supporting evidence reviewed, and any exceptions or waivers applied.
The full name of the individual or system process that performed the screening, for audit trail purposes.
The date on which the exclusion screening was performed.
The scheduled frequency or trigger for this screening event: monthly (CMS-required), quarterly, annual, ad hoc (event-driven), or initial credentialing.
The method or channel used to perform the exclusion screening: automated batch process, manual lookup by staff, real-time API integration, or third-party vendor service.

The outcome of the exclusion screening: Clear (no match), Match Found (confirmed exclusion), Potential Match (requires manual review), Inconclusive (insufficient data), or Error (screening system failure).

value_regex: OIG LEIE|SAM.gov|State Medicaid Exclusion|GSA EPLS|Multiple Sources
description: The authoritative exclusion list source(s) checked during this screening event (OIG List of Excluded Individuals and Entities, System for Award Management, state Medicaid exclusion lists, or General Services Administration Excluded Parties List System).
The name of the third-party vendor or service provider used to perform the exclusion screening, if applicable. Null for in-house screenings.
The unique identifier for this exclusion screening record in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this screening event must be reported to state Department of Insurance or Medicaid agencies per state-specific requirements.
The unique transaction or reference identifier assigned by the third-party screening vendor for this screening event, used for vendor reconciliation and support.
Reference to the screening event that detected this sanction. Links to the screening event record for audit trail purposes.
Reference to the provider who is subject to this sanction. Links to the provider master entity.
Free-text notes documenting the audit trail for this sanction record, including investigation details, stakeholder communications, and compliance actions taken.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Report Date
description: The date on which this sanction was reported to CMS. Null if not yet reported or not reportable.

business_glossary_term: Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether this sanction must be reported to CMS as part of program integrity reporting requirements. True if reportable, False otherwise.
Indicates whether this is the current active version of the sanction record. True for the current version, False for historical versions. Used for slowly changing dimension (SCD) Type 2 tracking.
Indicates whether a waiver or exception was granted allowing the provider to continue participation despite the sanction. True if waiver granted, False otherwise.
Detailed information about the match found during screening, including match score, matching fields (name, NPI, DOB), and any discrepancies requiring manual review.

business_glossary_term: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Report Date
description: The date on which this sanction was reported to NCQA. Null if not yet reported or not reportable.

business_glossary_term: National Committee for Quality Assurance (NCQA) Reportable Flag
description: Indicates whether this sanction must be reported to NCQA as part of credentialing and accreditation standards. True if reportable, False otherwise.
The date on which the sanction notification was sent to stakeholders. Null if notification has not been sent.
Indicates whether a notification was sent to relevant stakeholders (provider, network management, claims processing) about the sanction. True if sent, False otherwise.
The 10-digit National Provider Identifier of the sanctioned provider. Required for OIG and SAM screening compliance.

value_regex: Immediate Termination|Suspension Pending Review|Payment Hold|No Impact - Monitoring Only
description: The impact of the sanction on the provider's participation status and payment eligibility within the health plan's network.
Detailed explanation of the reason for the sanction, including the nature of the violation or adverse action (e.g., fraud, patient abuse, controlled substance violation).
The timestamp when this sanction record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The date from which this version of the sanction record is effective. Used for slowly changing dimension (SCD) Type 2 tracking.
The date on which this version of the sanction record ceased to be effective. Null for the current version. Used for slowly changing dimension (SCD) Type 2 tracking.
The timestamp when this sanction record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The date on which the provider was reinstated or the sanction was lifted. Null if the sanction is still active.

value_regex: Confirmed - Provider Terminated|Confirmed - Payment Hold|False Positive - No Action|Pending Investigation
description: The action taken by the credentialing or compliance team in response to the screening result and sanction identification.
The date on which the resolution action was completed and documented. Null if the sanction is still under investigation.
The date on which the sanction or exclusion was officially imposed by the sanctioning authority.

value_regex: OIG Exclusion|SAM Exclusion|State Medicaid Exclusion|DEA Revocation|Medical Board Action|Licensure Disciplinary Action
description: The category of sanction or exclusion imposed on the provider. Determines the scope and impact of the sanction on participation status.
Identifier of the staff member or automated system that performed the screening. May be a staff ID or system name (e.g., 'Automated Screening Service').
The date on which the screening was performed that identified this sanction. Required for monthly OIG/SAM screening compliance.

value_regex: Clear|Match Found|Potential Match - Manual Review Required
description: The outcome of the screening event: clear (no match), match found (confirmed sanction), or potential match requiring manual review.

value_regex: OIG LEIE|SAM.gov|State Medicaid Exclusion List|EPLS|Manual Review
description: The authoritative database or list consulted during the screening event (e.g., OIG LEIE, SAM.gov, state Medicaid exclusion lists).
The severity classification of the sanction, indicating the level of risk and impact on provider participation and payment eligibility.
The authoritative body or system that issued the sanction (e.g., OIG, SAM.gov, State Medicaid Agency, DEA, State Medical Board).
The unique identifier for this sanction record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The date on which the exclusion waiver expires and the sanction impact resumes. Null if no waiver granted or waiver is indefinite.
Explanation of the reason for granting an exclusion waiver, including business justification and regulatory approval details. Null if no waiver granted.
Unique identifier for the provider directory entry record. This is the primary key for the published directory entry, distinct from the internal provider master record.
Reference to the specific health plan for which this directory entry is published. A provider may have multiple directory entries across different plans.
Reference to the internal provider master record. Links this published directory entry to the authoritative provider identity in the provider domain.
Reference to the provider network in which this provider participates for this plan. Determines member access and cost-sharing tier.
Indicates whether the provider is currently accepting new patients for this plan and network. Critical for directory accuracy and member access.
Description of accessibility features available at the practice location (wheelchair access, hearing loop, accessible parking). Supports ADA compliance and member access.
Method used to verify the directory information with the provider. Documents the verification approach for audit and compliance purposes.
Status of the provider attestation or verification process for this directory entry. Tracks compliance with CMS directory accuracy requirements.
Comma-separated list of board certifications held by the provider. Demonstrates advanced training and specialty expertise.
Foreign key linking to contract.provider_contract. Business justification: Directory Publication Report requires contract details for each provider entry to determine network participation terms.
Consumer-facing display name of the provider as it appears in the published directory. May differ from legal name for readability and member experience.
Date when this directory entry was published or last updated in the member-facing provider directory. Required for CMS directory accuracy attestation.
Gender of the provider. Some members have preferences for provider gender, particularly for primary care and OB/GYN services.
Year the provider graduated from medical school or professional training program. Helps members assess provider experience.
Name of hospital(s) where the provider has admitting privileges or primary affiliation. Important for continuity of care and member decision-making.
Comma-separated list of languages spoken by the provider or available at the practice location. Critical for member access and cultural competency.
Date when the directory information was last verified with the provider or practice. CMS and state DOI mandate regular verification cycles (typically 90 days).
Name of the medical school or professional training institution where the provider received their degree. Provides credential transparency for members.
Cost-sharing tier for this provider within the plan network. Determines member copay, coinsurance, and out-of-pocket costs.
Date by which the next directory verification must be completed to maintain compliance with CMS and state DOI accuracy mandates.
Ten-digit National Provider Identifier assigned by CMS. The authoritative unique identifier for healthcare providers in the United States.
Text description of office hours for the practice location. Helps members understand availability for appointment scheduling.
Date when the providers participation in this plan and network became effective. Determines when members can access the provider at in-network rates.
Current status of the providers participation in this specific plan and network. Determines whether the directory entry is published to members.
Date when the providers participation in this plan and network ended or will end. Critical for member notification and continuity of care transitions.
Indicates whether this provider is eligible to serve as a Primary Care Provider (PCP) for plan members. Relevant for HMO and POS plans requiring PCP selection.
First line of the practice location street address where the provider sees patients. Published in member-facing directory for appointment scheduling.
Second line of the practice location street address (suite, floor, building). Optional field for additional address detail.
City name of the practice location. Used for member search and network adequacy geographic analysis.
County name of the practice location. Required for state DOI network adequacy reporting and CMS service area compliance.
Email address for the practice location. May be published for member communication or used for administrative coordination.
Fax number for the practice location. Used for prior authorization submissions and medical records exchange.
Name of the practice location, clinic, or facility where the provider sees patients for this directory entry. A provider may have multiple directory entries for different locations.
Primary phone number for the practice location. Published in directory for member appointment scheduling and inquiries.
Two-letter state abbreviation of the practice location. Critical for state-specific network adequacy and regulatory compliance.
Website URL for the practice or provider. Published in directory to enable members to learn more about the provider and services offered.
Five-digit or nine-digit ZIP code of the practice location. Used for member proximity search and network adequacy geo-access analysis.
High-level classification of the provider entity type. Determines directory presentation and search filtering options for members.
Timestamp when this directory entry record was first created in the data warehouse. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Timestamp when this directory entry record was last updated in the data warehouse. Tracks data freshness and change frequency.
Unique identifier for this directory entry in the source system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with upstream provider management systems.
Consumer-friendly specialty name displayed in the directory. Simplified from clinical taxonomy codes for member understanding.
Indicates whether the provider offers telehealth or virtual visit services for this plan. Increasingly important for member access and convenience.
Nine-digit Tax Identification Number (EIN or SSN) associated with the provider or practice for billing and contracting purposes.

business_glossary_term: National Provider Identifier (NPI) Registry Sync ID
description: Unique identifier for each NPI registry synchronization event record.
Internal identifier linking this sync event to the provider master record in the Provider Management System.
Detailed description of the address discrepancy found between internal and NPPES records, including field-level differences.
Indicates whether a discrepancy was detected between the internal provider address and the NPPES registry address during this sync event.
Comma-separated list of field names that were automatically updated in the internal provider master record based on NPPES data during this sync event.
Indicates whether discrepancies found during this sync event were automatically applied to the internal provider master record without manual review.
Indicates whether this sync event identified a risk to claims submission, such as a deactivated NPI that could cause claim rejections.
Detailed description of the credential discrepancy found between internal and NPPES records.
Indicates whether a discrepancy was detected between the internal provider credentials and the NPPES registry credentials during this sync event.
The CMS-defined code indicating the reason for NPI deactivation: DT (Death), DB (Doing Business As), FR (Fraud), OT (Other).
The full text description of the reason the NPI was deactivated in the NPPES registry.
Indicates whether this sync event identified discrepancies that impact provider directory accuracy and CMS directory accuracy mandates.
The user ID or team name to whom this sync discrepancy has been assigned for manual review and resolution.
The date on which manual review of this sync event discrepancy was completed and resolved.
The business reason why this sync event requires manual review, such as critical field discrepancies, deactivated NPI, or conflicting data.
Indicates whether this sync event identified discrepancies that require manual review and resolution by data stewardship or credentialing staff.

value_regex: Exact Match|Partial Match|Unmatched|Deactivated NPI|Reactivated NPI|New NPI Discovered
description: The outcome of comparing the internal provider record against the NPPES registry data, indicating whether the NPI was found, matched, or has changed status.
Detailed description of the name discrepancy found between internal and NPPES records, including field-level differences.
Indicates whether a discrepancy was detected between the internal provider name and the NPPES registry name during this sync event.
The 10-digit National Provider Identifier queried against the CMS NPPES registry during this synchronization event.

business_glossary_term: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Deactivation Date
description: The date the NPI was deactivated in the NPPES registry, if applicable, as retrieved during this sync event.

business_glossary_term: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Enumeration Date
description: The date the NPI was originally enumerated in the NPPES registry as retrieved during this sync event.

business_glossary_term: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Last Update Date
description: The date the NPI record was last updated in the NPPES registry as retrieved during this sync event.

business_glossary_term: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Reactivation Date
description: The date the NPI was reactivated in the NPPES registry, if applicable, as retrieved during this sync event.

business_glossary_term: National Plan and Provider Enumeration System (NPPES) Response Code
description: The HTTP or API response code returned by the NPPES registry during this sync event.
The timestamp when this NPI registry sync event record was first created in the data warehouse.
The timestamp when this NPI registry sync event record was last updated in the data warehouse.

value_regex: Accept NPPES Data|Retain Internal Data|Hybrid Update|Escalate to Credentialing|No Action Required
description: The action taken to resolve discrepancies identified during this sync event, indicating whether NPPES data was accepted, internal data was retained, or further escalation occurred.
Free-text notes documenting the rationale and details of the resolution action taken for this sync event.
The unique identifier for this sync event record in the source Provider Management System.
The error code returned if the sync event failed, indicating the technical or business reason for failure.
The detailed error message returned if the sync event failed, providing diagnostic information for troubleshooting.
The date on which this NPI registry synchronization event was executed.
The precise timestamp when this NPI registry synchronization event was initiated.

value_regex: NPPES Weekly Dissemination File|NPPES API Real-Time Query|Manual Verification|Batch Import|Scheduled Job
description: The source system or method used to retrieve NPI data from the CMS NPPES registry for this synchronization event.
The current status of this NPI registry synchronization event in its processing lifecycle.
Detailed description of the taxonomy code discrepancy found between internal and NPPES records, including specific taxonomy codes that differ.
Indicates whether a discrepancy was detected between the internal provider taxonomy code and the NPPES registry taxonomy during this sync event.
The total number of field-level discrepancies identified between the internal provider record and the NPPES registry data during this sync event.
System‑generated unique identifier for each provider directory verification record.
Identifier of the staff member or automated system that performed the verification.
Unique identifier of the provider whose directory information is being verified.
Indicates whether this row represents the current active version of the verification record.
Pipe‑separated list of provider directory fields that were verified in this event (e.g., address|phone|specialty|accepting_patients).
Scheduled future date for the next required verification of this provider's directory information.
Free‑form comments or observations captured during the verification process.
Timestamp when the verification record was first inserted into the lakehouse.
Date from which this version of the verification record is considered effective (SCD Type 2).
Date when this version of the verification record ceased to be effective; null if current.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the verification record.
Native identifier of the verification record in the source system.
Channel used to conduct the verification (e.g., phone call, mailed questionnaire, online portal, provider attestation).
Business‑visible sequential number assigned to the verification event for tracking and reporting.
Result of the verification activity indicating whether the record was confirmed, required an update, could not be reached, or the provider was removed from the directory.
Current lifecycle status of the verification record.
Exact date‑time when the verification activity was performed.

Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Affiliation coordination is handled by a specific employee who manages contracts, privileges, and network status for each affiliation.

Foreign key linking to provider.group_practice. Business justification: affiliation should reference the medical group (group_practice) it belongs to; the existing column name does not match target PK naming, so a new FK column is added and the generic column removed.
Reference to the individual provider (physician, nurse practitioner, or other healthcare professional) who holds this affiliation. Links to the provider master entity.
Boolean indicator denoting whether the provider is accepting new patients through this affiliation. True indicates accepting new patients; False indicates not accepting new patients.

Boolean indicator denoting whether the provider has admitting privileges at the affiliated hospital or facility. True indicates admitting privileges are granted; False indicates no admitting privileges.
Current lifecycle status of the provider affiliation. Indicates whether the affiliation is active and in effect, inactive, suspended, pending approval, or terminated.

Categorical classification of the affiliation relationship. Indicates whether the provider is affiliated with a medical group, Independent Practice Association (IPA), Accountable Care Organization (ACO), health system, hospital, or other facility type.
The National Provider Identifier (NPI) used for billing purposes in this affiliation context. May represent the organizational NPI (Type 2) of the affiliated entity.
The date and time when this affiliation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The date on which the provider's credentials and affiliation with the organization were last verified. Used to track compliance with credentialing re-verification requirements.

The name of the department, division, or service line within the affiliated organization where the provider practices. Examples include Cardiology, Emergency Medicine, Orthopedic Surgery, Primary Care.

Boolean indicator denoting whether this affiliation should be displayed in the member-facing provider directory. True indicates the affiliation is publicly visible; False indicates it is for internal use only.
The date on which the provider affiliation ended or is scheduled to end. Null for open-ended affiliations that are currently active.
The user ID or system identifier of the person or process that last updated this affiliation record. Supports audit and accountability requirements.
The date and time when this affiliation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.

Unique surrogate identifier for the capitation arrangement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capitation arrangements are budgeted and tracked via a cost center to manage per‑member‑per‑month payments and related expenses.
Reference to the health plan or benefit plan product associated with this capitation arrangement. Determines the member population eligible for attribution.
Reference to the capitated provider or provider group receiving the Per Member Per Month (PMPM) payment under this arrangement.
Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: CAPITATION_PAYMENT: Each capitation arrangement draws funding from a designated risk pool for compliance and financial reporting.

Indicates whether this capitation arrangement is part of an Accountable Care Organization (ACO) or integrated delivery network model, which may have additional shared savings or risk-sharing components.

Total dollar amount for the attributed member population above which the health plan assumes financial responsibility for claims costs. Protects the provider from population-level adverse risk. Expressed in USD.
Identifier of the most recent contract amendment that modified this capitation arrangement. Tracks the amendment history for audit and compliance purposes.
Contractually agreed annual percentage increase applied to the PMPM rate at each rate renewal period (e.g., 3.50 for 3.5% annual escalation). Used for multi-year capitation arrangements.
Date on which the capitation arrangement was formally approved by the health plan's contracting authority. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance documentation.
Name or employee identifier of the contracting authority who approved this capitation arrangement. Supports audit trail and delegation-of-authority compliance.
Human-readable descriptive name for the capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care Capitation - HMO Commercial 2024'), used in reporting and provider communications.

Externally-known business identifier for the capitation arrangement, used in provider communications, payment remittances, and reconciliation processes. Assigned by the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).
Current lifecycle status of the capitation arrangement. Controls whether PMPM payments are generated and whether members can be attributed to this arrangement.

Classification of the capitation arrangement by scope of covered services. Global capitation covers all services; primary care capitation covers PCP services only; specialty capitation covers specialist services; institutional covers facility-based care. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: global_capitation|primary_care_capitation|specialty_capitation|institutional_capitation|behavioral_health_capitation|pharmacy_capitation — promote to reference product]
Current number of members attributed to this capitation arrangement for the active payment period. Used to calculate total monthly capitation payment disbursement.

Methodology used to attribute members to this capitation arrangement. PCP assignment uses enrollment data; claims-based uses plurality of visits; voluntary selection allows member choice; geographic assigns by service area.
Indicates whether this capitation arrangement automatically renews at the end of the contract term unless either party provides notice of termination within the contractually specified notice period.

Description of services explicitly excluded from the capitation scope and reimbursed separately (e.g., 'Mental Health, Substance Abuse, Transplants, Neonatal ICU'). Carve-outs are paid fee-for-service or under separate capitation arrangements.
Reference to the parent provider contract under which this capitation arrangement is established. Links to the contract master record.
Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and regulatory compliance requirements.
Date on which the capitation arrangement becomes binding and PMPM payments begin. Aligns with the contract effective period.
Description or code of the geographic service area (county, state, or region) within which this capitation arrangement applies. Members outside this area may not be attributed to this arrangement.
Dollar amount per member per year above which the health plan assumes financial responsibility for claims costs, protecting the provider from catastrophic individual member costs. Expressed in USD.
Line of Business to which this capitation arrangement applies (e.g., Commercial HMO, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid Managed Care). Determines regulatory requirements and rate-setting methodology.

Network tier classification of the provider under this capitation arrangement. Tier 1 providers typically receive higher capitation rates in exchange for meeting quality and cost-efficiency benchmarks.

Frequency at which capitation payments are disbursed to the provider. Monthly is standard for most capitation arrangements; quarterly or annual may apply for certain specialty or institutional arrangements.

Specific payment calculation methodology applied to this capitation arrangement. Pure capitation pays flat PMPM; withhold arrangements retain a percentage pending performance; risk-adjusted capitation applies RAF multipliers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pure_capitation|capitation_with_withhold|capitation_with_bonus|risk_adjusted_capitation|tiered_capitation — promote to reference product]
Fixed dollar amount paid to the provider per attributed member per calendar month, regardless of services rendered. The core financial parameter of the capitation arrangement. Expressed in USD.

business_glossary_term: Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate Currency
description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the PMPM rate (typically USD for domestic health plans). Required for multi-currency reporting and regulatory submissions.

Description of the quality performance benchmarks or HEDIS/STAR measure thresholds that must be met for the provider to receive release of withheld capitation amounts. Defines the performance contract terms.

Multiplier applied to the base PMPM rate to account for the relative health risk of the attributed member population. A RAF above 1.0 indicates a sicker-than-average population; below 1.0 indicates healthier-than-average.
Date on which the current PMPM rate became effective. Supports rate history tracking when rates are renegotiated mid-contract without creating a new arrangement record.
Date on which the current PMPM rate expires and is subject to renegotiation or automatic escalation. Null if the rate is open-ended for the arrangement term.

Indicates whether the PMPM rate is subject to risk adjustment based on the attributed member population's Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) scores. Applicable primarily for Medicare Advantage and ACA Exchange arrangements.
Indicates whether the provider participates in a shared risk pool under this capitation arrangement. Risk pool participants share in surpluses or deficits relative to the capitation budget.
Percentage of risk pool surplus or deficit allocated to the provider under this capitation arrangement. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Applicable only when risk_pool_participant is True.

Comma-delimited list or narrative description of the covered service categories included under this capitation arrangement (e.g., 'Primary Care, Preventive Services, Lab, Radiology'). Defines the scope of services the provider is financially responsible for under capitation.

Code identifying the operational system of record from which this capitation arrangement record was sourced (e.g., FACETS, QNXT for Core Administration Platform; CACTUS or PROVIDER_SOURCE for Provider Management System).

Native record identifier from the operational source system (Facets, QNXT, or Provider Management System) for this capitation arrangement. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and back-referencing to the system of record.

Type of stop-loss protection included in the capitation arrangement. Individual stop-loss limits exposure per member; aggregate stop-loss limits total population exposure; both applies both thresholds.
Date on which the capitation arrangement ends and PMPM payments cease. Null for open-ended arrangements. Distinct from contract termination date when partial termination applies.
Number of calendar days advance notice required by either party to terminate this capitation arrangement without cause. Typically 90–180 days for managed care capitation contracts.
Timestamp when this capitation arrangement record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture, incremental processing, and audit trail maintenance.

Indicates whether this capitation arrangement includes Value-Based Care (VBC) performance incentive components such as quality bonuses, shared savings, or outcome-based adjustments beyond the base PMPM.

Percentage of the PMPM rate withheld from each payment cycle pending performance or quality metric achievement. Withheld amounts are returned upon meeting contractual benchmarks. Expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00).
Unique surrogate identifier for each capitation payment disbursement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Ensures each capitation payment is tied to the employee who authorized it, supporting payment audit and regulatory compliance.
Reference to the specific capitation arrangement or rate schedule within the contract, identifying the applicable PMPM rate table, risk pool configuration, and attributed population definition.
Reference to the health plan or benefit product under which the attributed members are enrolled. Determines the applicable benefit structure and capitation rate tier.

Reference to the original capitation payment record that this record adjusts, reverses, or supplements. Populated only when prior_period_adjustment_flag is True. Enables audit trail linking of adjustment chains.

Accounts Payable (AP) voucher or invoice number assigned in Oracle Financials when the capitation payment is posted as a payable. Links the capitation disbursement to the AP subledger for financial reporting and audit trail.

Total number of members attributed to the provider for the payment period. This is the primary driver of the gross capitation payment calculation (attributed_member_count × pmpm_rate = gross_payment_amount).

Rate tier or member category applied to determine the PMPM rate for this payment (e.g., adult, pediatric, senior, ESRD, dual-eligible). Different member demographics may attract different PMPM rates under the same contract.

Check number or EFT/ACH trace number assigned by the financial institution for the actual disbursement of this capitation payment. Used for bank reconciliation in Oracle Financials (AP module) and provider payment dispute resolution.
Reference to the capitation contract under which this payment is made. Links to the provider contract master defining the capitation arrangement, PMPM rates, and risk-sharing terms.
Timestamp when this capitation payment record was first created in the Silver Layer data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA monitoring for payment processing cycles.

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this capitation payment record. Typically USD for domestic health insurance operations. Required for multi-currency financial reconciliation and GAAP/SAP reporting.

Dollar adjustment to the gross payment resulting from retroactive enrollment changes (late enrollments, disenrollments, or eligibility corrections) identified after the initial payment calculation. Can be positive (additional payment) or negative (recoupment).

General Ledger (GL) cost center code in Oracle Financials to which this capitation payment expense is allocated. Supports financial reporting by LOB, product, and organizational unit per GAAP and SAP statutory reporting requirements.

Total gross capitation payment amount before any adjustments, calculated as attributed_member_count multiplied by the pmpm_rate. Represents the base payment obligation for the period prior to retroactive enrollment changes, risk pool withholds, or stop-loss recoveries.
Line of Business (LOB) under which the capitation payment is made. Determines regulatory requirements, rate-setting methodology, and CMS reporting obligations applicable to the payment.

Final net amount disbursed to the provider after all adjustments: gross_payment_amount + enrollment_adjustment_amount - risk_pool_withhold_amount + stop_loss_recovery_amount + other_adjustment_amount. This is the actual amount reflected in the 835 ERA remittance advice.

Catch-all adjustment amount for miscellaneous contractual adjustments not captured in enrollment, risk pool, or stop-loss categories. Includes quality bonus payments, performance incentive adjustments, or administrative fee offsets per contract terms.

Date on which the capitation payment was formally approved for disbursement by the authorized approver in the billing or finance system. Supports segregation of duties controls and audit trail requirements.
Actual date the capitation payment was disbursed to the provider via EFT, check, or ACH. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and provider remittance reporting.
Mechanism by which the capitation payment was or will be disbursed to the provider. EFT (Electronic Funds Transfer) and ACH (Automated Clearing House) are most common for capitation arrangements.

Externally-visible business identifier for this capitation payment disbursement, used in provider remittance advice (835 ERA), accounts payable records, and provider portal references. Format: CAP-YYYYMM-NNNNNN.
Last day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the last day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.
First day of the coverage month or period for which this capitation payment is being made. Typically the first day of the calendar month in monthly capitation cycles.

Identifier of the capitation payment run or batch cycle that generated this payment record. Used to group all payments processed in the same cycle for reconciliation, audit, and reprocessing purposes in the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT).

Current lifecycle state of the capitation payment disbursement. 'pending' = calculated but not yet approved; 'approved' = authorized for payment; 'paid' = disbursed to provider; 'voided' = cancelled before disbursement; 'reversed' = clawed back after disbursement; 'on_hold' = suspended pending investigation or contract dispute.

Classification of the capitation payment by its business purpose. 'standard' = routine monthly capitation; 'retroactive' = payment for prior periods due to enrollment changes; 'adjustment' = correction to a previously issued payment; 'stop_loss_recovery' = reimbursement under stop-loss provisions; 'risk_pool_settlement' = periodic risk pool distribution or withhold settlement.

Per Member Per Month (PMPM) capitation rate applied for this payment period, as defined in the contract rate schedule. This is the contractually negotiated amount paid per attributed member per month. Classified confidential as it represents proprietary contract pricing.

Indicates whether this capitation payment includes or represents an adjustment to a prior payment period. True when the payment corrects or supplements a previously issued capitation payment due to retroactive enrollment changes or rate corrections.

Amount withheld from the capitation payment pending achievement of quality performance targets (e.g., HEDIS measures, STAR ratings thresholds). Released or forfeited at the end of the performance measurement period based on provider quality scores.

Reference number of the 835 Electronic Remittance Advice (ERA) transaction sent to the provider for this capitation payment. Used to reconcile capitation disbursements with provider accounts receivable and the EDI clearinghouse (Availity/Change Healthcare) transaction log.

business_glossary_term: Risk-Adjusted Per Member Per Month (PMPM) Rate
description: PMPM rate after application of the risk adjustment factor (RAF score) to the base contractual PMPM rate. Reflects the risk-adjusted capitation amount per member per month for Medicare Advantage or risk-adjusted commercial arrangements.

Amount withheld from the gross capitation payment and placed into a shared risk pool per the contract terms. Risk pool withholds are held and distributed (or forfeited) at the end of the risk settlement period based on actual utilization versus targets.

Aggregate Risk Adjustment Factor (RAF) score for the attributed member population used to adjust the capitation rate for this payment period. Derived from Hierarchical Condition Category (HCC) coding and CMS Risk Adjustment Processing System (RAPS/EDPS) submissions.

Native identifier of this capitation payment record in the originating operational system (Facets, QNXT, or HealthEdge). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver Layer and the system of record.

Amount recovered from or credited to the provider under stop-loss provisions of the capitation contract. Positive values indicate plan reimbursement to provider for catastrophic cases exceeding the stop-loss threshold; negative values indicate provider liability adjustments.

Timestamp when this capitation payment record was last modified in the Silver Layer data platform. Tracks the most recent update for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and reconciliation purposes.

Indicates whether this capitation payment is made under a Value-Based Care (VBC) or Accountable Care Organization (ACO) contract arrangement, as opposed to a standard fee-for-service capitation. Drives VBC performance reporting and quality incentive calculations.

Business reason code or description explaining why this capitation payment was voided or reversed. Populated only when payment_status is 'voided' or 'reversed'. Examples: contract_termination, duplicate_payment, enrollment_error, rate_correction.
Unique surrogate identifier for the fee schedule record in the contract domain. Primary key for the fee_schedule data product.
Reference to the specific health plan or benefit plan to which this fee schedule is scoped. Enables plan-level fee schedule assignment in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.
Reference to the provider or provider group for whom this fee schedule defines reimbursement rates. Sourced from the Provider Management System (Cactus/ProviderSource).
Reference to the provider network (e.g., HMO, PPO, EPO, POS) to which this fee schedule applies. Determines in-network vs. out-of-network reimbursement applicability.
Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: RATE_DEVELOPMENT: Fee schedules are applied within a specific risk pool; linking enables actuarial rate calculations and regulatory filings.
Reference to the fee_schedule_id of the newer fee schedule that replaced this one when schedule_status is 'superseded'. Enables fee schedule version chain navigation and historical rate lookups.

Identifier for the contract amendment that introduced or modified this fee schedule version. Null for the original fee schedule. Enables traceability between fee schedule versions and contract amendments.
Indicates whether this fee schedule includes anesthesia service reimbursement, which uses base units plus time units multiplied by a conversion factor rather than standard CPT-based rates.
Dollar amount per anesthesia unit (base units + time units) used to calculate anesthesia reimbursement when anesthesia_applicable is True. Negotiated rate specific to this fee schedule.
Date on which the fee schedule was formally approved by the authorized contracting officer or governance body. Required for audit and regulatory compliance.

The CMS Medicare fee schedule calendar year used as the basis or benchmark for this fee schedule when fee_schedule_source is 'cms_medicare'. Enables tracking of which CMS annual update cycle the rates are derived from.
Reference to the provider contract under which this fee schedule is established. Links the fee schedule to its governing contractual agreement.

Dollar amount multiplied by the total Relative Value Units (RVUs) to calculate the reimbursement amount for RVU-based fee schedules. Applicable when payment_basis is 'rvu_based'. Sourced from CMS RBRVS conversion factor or negotiated contract rate.
Indicates whether Coordination of Benefits (COB) rules apply to claims adjudicated under this fee schedule. When True, COB logic determines primary vs. secondary payer responsibilities.

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range End
description: Ending CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_start to define the scope of applicable procedure codes.

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology (CPT) Code Range Start
description: Starting CPT procedure code in the range of services covered by this fee schedule. Used in conjunction with cpt_code_range_end to define the scope of applicable procedure codes. Null if the fee schedule applies to all CPT codes or uses ICD/DRG-based scoping.
Timestamp when this fee schedule record was first created in the system. Audit field capturing the initial record creation event in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Indicates whether this fee schedule uses Diagnosis Related Group (DRG)-based reimbursement for inpatient facility claims. When True, DRG weights and base rates govern payment rather than individual procedure codes.

The negotiated base dollar rate per DRG weight unit used for inpatient facility reimbursement when drg_applicable is True. Multiplied by the DRG relative weight to calculate the total inpatient payment.
Date on which the fee schedule becomes effective and applicable for claim adjudication and reimbursement calculations. Inclusive start date of the fee schedule validity period.
Free-text narrative description of the fee schedule, including its purpose, scope, special provisions, or notes relevant to adjudication staff and contract analysts.

Indicates whether geographic adjustment factors (e.g., CMS Geographic Practice Cost Indices - GPCI) are applied to rates under this fee schedule. True when locality-based adjustments are in effect; False for flat national rates.
Indicates whether global surgery payment rules apply under this fee schedule, bundling pre-operative, intra-operative, and post-operative services into a single reimbursement amount.

Identifies the Line of Business (LOB) to which this fee schedule applies (e.g., commercial, Medicare Advantage, Medicaid, ACA Marketplace, COBRA, FEHB). Determines which member populations and plan types are subject to this fee schedule during adjudication.
CMS Medicare locality code identifying the geographic pricing area applicable to this fee schedule when geographic_modifier_applicable is True. Used to apply GPCI adjustments to RVU-based rates.

Indicates whether this fee schedule applies to Medicare crossover claims where Medicare is the primary payer and the health plan is the secondary payer. Relevant for Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans.

Indicates whether CPT or HCPCS modifiers affect reimbursement rates under this fee schedule (e.g., modifier 26 for professional component, modifier TC for technical component, modifier 50 for bilateral procedures).

Dollar threshold above which a claim qualifies as a cost outlier, triggering additional reimbursement beyond the standard DRG or bundled payment. Applicable for institutional fee schedules with outlier payment provisions.

The methodology used to calculate reimbursement amounts under this fee schedule. Options include: pct_medicare (percentage of Medicare allowable), pct_billed_charges (percentage of provider billed charges), flat_rate (fixed dollar amount per service), rvu_based (Relative Value Unit-based), bundled (bundled payment for episode of care), capitation (per-member per-month), rbp (Reference Based Pricing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pct_medicare|pct_billed_charges|flat_rate|rvu_based|bundled|capitation|rbp — promote to reference product]

The percentage rate applied when payment_basis is 'pct_medicare' or 'pct_billed_charges' (e.g., 110.0000 for 110% of Medicare). Null when payment_basis is flat_rate, rvu_based, bundled, or capitation. Stored as a decimal percentage value.
Indicates whether Prior Authorization (PA) is required for services reimbursed under this fee schedule. When True, claims must have an approved PA on file before adjudication at the scheduled rates.
Indicates whether a primary care physician (PCP) referral is required for services covered under this fee schedule. Applicable primarily for HMO and POS plan types.
Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the fee schedule within the Core Administration Platform (Facets/QNXT). Used in EDI transactions and cross-system references.

Human-readable name identifying the fee schedule (e.g., 'Commercial PPO Professional 2024', 'Medicare Advantage Institutional Q1 2024'). Used for display and reporting in Facets/QNXT Benefits Configuration.

Current lifecycle state of the fee schedule. 'draft' indicates under review; 'active' indicates currently in use for adjudication; 'suspended' indicates temporarily inactive; 'terminated' indicates permanently closed; 'superseded' indicates replaced by a newer version.

Categorizes the fee schedule by the type of services covered: professional (physician/practitioner), institutional (hospital/facility), ancillary (therapy/home health), DME (Durable Medical Equipment), lab (laboratory), or pharmacy. Drives claim adjudication logic in Facets/QNXT.

Broad clinical or administrative category of services covered by this fee schedule (e.g., 'Evaluation and Management', 'Surgery', 'Radiology', 'Pathology', 'Inpatient Facility', 'Outpatient Facility', 'Home Health', 'Behavioral Health'). Supports fee schedule segmentation and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: evaluation_management|surgery|radiology|pathology|inpatient_facility|outpatient_facility|home_health|behavioral_health|physical_therapy|dme — promote to reference product]

Identifies the origin or benchmark source of the fee schedule rates: cms_medicare (based on CMS Medicare fee schedule), cms_medicaid (based on state Medicaid fee schedule), negotiated (directly negotiated with provider), custom (health plan proprietary rates), rbp_benchmark (Reference Based Pricing benchmark).

Two-letter US state code indicating the geographic scope of this fee schedule. Used for state-specific rate variations and regulatory compliance with State Departments of Insurance (DOI) requirements.
Indicates whether a stop-loss provision applies to this fee schedule, capping the health plan's reimbursement liability per claim or per member. Relevant for risk-sharing and capitation arrangements.
Dollar amount per claim or per member above which the stop-loss provision activates, limiting the health plan's reimbursement exposure. Applicable when stop_loss_applicable is True.
Date on which the fee schedule expires and is no longer applicable for claim adjudication. Null indicates an open-ended fee schedule with no defined end date.
Timestamp when this fee schedule record was last modified. Audit field supporting change tracking, data lineage, and regulatory audit requirements.

Version identifier for the fee schedule, enabling tracking of amendments and updates over time (e.g., '1.0', '2.1', '3.0'). Incremented when rates or terms are modified. Supports fee schedule history and audit trail.
Unique surrogate identifier for each fee schedule rate line record in the contract domain. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the parent fee schedule header that this rate line belongs to. Links the rate to its governing fee schedule agreement.

Self-referencing identifier pointing to the previous version of this rate record that was superseded by the current record. Enables full rate history chain traversal for audit and compliance purposes.

The contracted allowed amount or maximum reimbursable dollar amount for the specified procedure code, modifier, and place of service combination. This is the principal monetary value used by adjudication systems to price claims against contracted rates. Expressed in USD.

When rate_derivation_method is AWP_PCT, this field stores the percentage of the Average Wholesale Price (AWP) used to derive the drug or supply reimbursement rate. For example, 0.8500 represents AWP minus 15%. Commonly used in pharmacy and DME fee schedules.

Indicates whether this rate applies to a bilateral procedure (performed on both sides of the body). When true, the allowed amount may be adjusted per CMS bilateral surgery payment rules (150% of the unilateral rate).

Single episode-of-care payment amount covering all services within a defined clinical episode (e.g., joint replacement, maternity). Applicable when payment_methodology is BUNDLED. Supports Value-Based Care (VBC) and ACO payment models.

business_glossary_term: Capitation Rate Per Member Per Month (PMPM)
description: Fixed monthly payment per enrolled member paid to a provider under a capitation arrangement, regardless of services rendered. Expressed as a Per Member Per Month (PMPM) dollar amount. Applicable only when payment_methodology is CAPITATION.
Reference to the provider contract under which this fee schedule rate is established. Links the rate line to the governing contractual agreement.

The timestamp when this fee schedule rate record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).

CMS Diagnosis Related Group code used for inpatient hospital reimbursement. When the payment methodology is DRG-based, this code identifies the patient classification group that determines the base payment rate.

The date on which this fee schedule rate becomes effective and applicable for claims adjudication. Claims with service dates on or after this date and before the termination_date will be priced using this rate.

Geographic region or market area to which this rate applies. Rates may vary by state, CMS locality, metropolitan statistical area (MSA), or health plan-defined region. Expressed as a state code, CMS locality code, or internal region identifier.

Number of days in the global surgery period for surgical procedure codes. Defines the pre-operative and post-operative period during which related services are bundled into the surgical payment (0=endoscopic/minor, 10=minor, 90=major surgery).

The Line of Business (LOB) to which this fee schedule rate applies. Rates are often differentiated by LOB as Medicare, Medicaid, and commercial plans have distinct reimbursement structures and regulatory requirements.

The Maximum Allowable Cost (MAC) dollar amount set as the ceiling for reimbursement of a specific drug or service. When rate_derivation_method is MAC, this value represents the maximum the plan will pay regardless of billed charges.
Maximum number of units of this procedure code that will be reimbursed per day per member. Used to enforce utilization limits during claims adjudication and prevent overbilling.
Maximum number of units of this procedure code that will be reimbursed per benefit year per member. Enforces annual utilization limits during adjudication for services with frequency restrictions.

When rate_derivation_method is MEDICARE_PCT, this field stores the percentage of the applicable Medicare fee schedule rate used to derive the allowed amount. For example, 1.1500 represents 115% of Medicare. Used for rate benchmarking and contract analytics.

business_glossary_term: National Correct Coding Initiative (NCCI) Edit Indicator
description: Indicates whether this procedure code is subject to National Correct Coding Initiative (NCCI) bundling edits during claims adjudication. When true, the adjudication system will apply NCCI column 1/column 2 and mutually exclusive code edits.

Identifies the provider network tier to which this rate applies. Rates differ by network participation level: IN_NETWORK (contracted), OUT_OF_NETWORK (non-contracted), or tiered preferred network levels (TIER_1, TIER_2, TIER_3, PREFERRED). Drives member cost-sharing calculations.

The reimbursement methodology governing how the provider is paid for this service. FFS=Fee-for-Service (fixed allowed amount per procedure), CAPITATION=fixed PMPM regardless of utilization, BUNDLED=single payment for episode of care, DRG=Diagnosis Related Group inpatient payment, PER_DIEM=daily rate for facility stays, PERCENT_OF_BILLED=percentage of submitted charges, RBP=Reference Based Pricing, CASE_RATE=flat rate per case. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FFS|CAPITATION|BUNDLED|DRG|PER_DIEM|PERCENT_OF_BILLED|RBP|CASE_RATE — promote to reference product]

Daily reimbursement rate paid to a facility provider for each day of an inpatient or residential stay. Applicable when payment_methodology is PER_DIEM. May vary by level of care (e.g., ICU vs. medical/surgical).

CMS two-digit place of service code indicating the setting where the service was rendered (e.g., 11=Office, 21=Inpatient Hospital, 22=Outpatient Hospital, 23=Emergency Room). Rates may vary by place of service.

business_glossary_term: Current Procedural Terminology / Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (CPT/HCPCS) Procedure Code
description: The CPT or HCPCS procedure code identifying the specific medical service, procedure, or supply for which this rate applies. Core identifier used in claims adjudication to match services to contracted rates.

Classifies the coding system used for the procedure_code field. Distinguishes between CPT (physician services), HCPCS Level II (supplies/DME), CDT (dental), ICD-10-PCS (inpatient procedures), Revenue Code (facility billing), DRG (diagnosis-related group), APC (ambulatory payment classification), or NDC (drug). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPT|HCPCS_II|CDT|ICD_PCS|REVENUE|DRG|APC|NDC — promote to reference product]

First CPT or HCPCS modifier code appended to the procedure code to indicate special circumstances affecting the service (e.g., bilateral procedure, assistant surgeon, professional component). Modifiers can alter the allowed reimbursement amount.
Second CPT or HCPCS modifier code when multiple modifiers apply to the same procedure code. Used in conjunction with procedure_modifier_1 for complex billing scenarios.

The health plan product type to which this rate applies. HMO=Health Maintenance Organization, PPO=Preferred Provider Organization, EPO=Exclusive Provider Organization, POS=Point of Service, HDHP=High Deductible Health Plan, INDEMNITY=traditional indemnity plan. Rates may vary by product type.

Classification of the provider type to which this rate applies. Distinguishes between physician/professional services, facility (hospital/SNF), ancillary services, Durable Medical Equipment (DME) suppliers, pharmacy, and behavioral health providers.

Describes how the allowed amount was derived or calculated. MEDICARE_PCT=percentage of Medicare fee schedule, AWP_PCT=percentage of Average Wholesale Price, WAC_PCT=percentage of Wholesale Acquisition Cost, MAC=Maximum Allowable Cost, RBP=Reference Based Pricing benchmark, NEGOTIATED=directly negotiated flat rate, COST_PLUS=cost plus margin, BILLED_PCT=percent of billed charges. Critical for audit and rate validation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MEDICARE_PCT|AWP_PCT|WAC_PCT|MAC|RBP|NEGOTIATED|COST_PLUS|BILLED_PCT — promote to reference product]
Sequential line number identifying the position of this rate record within the parent fee schedule. Used for ordering and referencing individual rate lines.

Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about this fee schedule rate, such as special billing instructions, exceptions, or conditions under which the rate applies. Used by contracting and claims operations staff.

Reimbursement rate expressed as a percentage of the provider's billed charges, used when the payment methodology is percent-of-billed rather than a fixed allowed amount. For example, 0.8500 represents 85% of billed charges.

Identifies the origin or source of this fee schedule rate. CONTRACT=directly negotiated in provider contract, CMS_PUBLISHED=derived from published CMS fee schedule, CROSSWALK=mapped from another code or schedule, MANUAL=manually entered by contracting staff, SYSTEM_GENERATED=automatically generated by the fee schedule management system.

Current lifecycle status of this fee schedule rate record. ACTIVE=currently in effect and used for adjudication, INACTIVE=not currently in use, PENDING=approved but not yet effective, SUPERSEDED=replaced by a newer rate version, TERMINATED=permanently ended.

Incrementing version number tracking amendments and updates to this fee schedule rate line. Version 1 is the original rate; subsequent amendments increment this counter. Supports rate history and audit trail requirements.

The benchmark dollar amount used as the basis for Reference Based Pricing (RBP) reimbursement. Typically anchored to Medicare rates or a cost-based benchmark. The actual allowed amount is derived as a percentage of this reference amount.

Four-digit UB-04 revenue code identifying the type of accommodation, ancillary service, or billing classification for facility claims. Used in conjunction with or as an alternative to procedure codes for hospital and facility fee schedule rates.

Broad clinical or administrative category grouping the service type covered by this rate (e.g., Evaluation and Management, Surgery, Radiology, Laboratory, Anesthesia, Durable Medical Equipment, Behavioral Health, Preventive Care). Used for rate analytics and reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]

CMS three-digit provider specialty code indicating the clinical specialty for which this rate applies. Rates may be differentiated by provider specialty (e.g., 01=General Practice, 06=Cardiology, 93=Emergency Medicine). Null if rate applies to all specialties.
Two-letter US state code identifying the state jurisdiction in which this fee schedule rate is applicable. Used for state-specific rate differentiation and regulatory compliance reporting.

The date on which this fee schedule rate expires and is no longer applicable for claims adjudication. Null indicates the rate is open-ended with no defined end date. Claims with service dates after this date will not be priced using this rate.

The number of units of service to which the allowed_amount applies. For most procedure codes, this is 1 unit. For time-based codes or quantity-based services, this may represent the standard unit quantity (e.g., 15-minute increments for therapy codes).

The timestamp when this fee schedule rate record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update for change management, audit trail, and data synchronization purposes. Conforms to ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).
Unique surrogate identifier for the reimbursement policy record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the fee schedule that serves as the base rate table for this reimbursement policy. The policy reduction or adjustment is applied against the rates defined in the referenced fee schedule.
Foreign key linking to risk.pool. Business justification: POLICY_COMPLIANCE: Policies may vary by risk pool; linking supports audit trails and CMS risk‑adjustment audits.

Reference to the prior reimbursement policy record that this policy replaces or supersedes. Enables policy lineage tracking and ensures historical adjudication records can be traced to the correct policy version.
